fr-CA.Reports.NoiseAttenuationStatement.Title: Déclaration d'atténuation du bruit
fr-CA.Reports.NoiseAttenuationStatement.Body:
1. TRAVAUX D’ATTÉNUATION DU BRUIT

L’entente du plan d’aménagement du projet pour les terrains (the “Site Plan Agreement”) exige du propriétaire d’effectuer les travaux d’atténuation du bruit pour les logements suivants sur le terrain (The “Unit Lands”):

1.1 Climatisation centrale pour les logements situés au :

  •  Tous les logements résidentiels des deux tours

Le niveau de bruit du condenseur à refroidissement par air ne doit pas être supérieur à 7,6 dB et l’appareil doit être installé à un endroit où son bruit a le moins d’impact sur les activités extérieures des occupant(e)s et du voisinage.

1.2    Des fenêtres acoustiques affichant un indice de transmission du son entre 17 et 37 doivent être installées dans les logements, comme indiqué dans le rapport de la firme de génie-conseil SS Wilson Associates’ Rapport détaillé sur le bruit, 8e révision, daté du 6 mars 2019.

 

2. CLAUSES D’AVERTISSEMENT

Le plan d’aménagement du projet exige du propriétaire de joindre un exemplaire des clauses d’avertissement suivantes à toutes les ententes de vente ou d’achat de ces logements ou d’envoyer un exemplaire de ces clauses d’avertissement à l’acheteur des logements avant l’exécution de l’acte d’achat ou de vente :

2.1 Terrains touchés :

Comme il est prévu à la section 1, 1.2 de la présente déclaration :

Clause d’avertissement :

“Les acheteurs sont informés que malgré l’inclusion de mesures de contrôle du bruit pour la présente zone de développement et au sein des logements, le niveau de bruit occasionné par un trafic ferroviaire en hausse continue de perturber à l’occasion les activités des occupant(e)s, car il peut excéder la norme ht3 de la municipalité et du ministère de l’Environnement, de la Conservation et des Parcs. Par la présente, je, l’acheteur, conviens d’inclure la présente clause à toute offre d’achat ou de vente ultérieure à la vente de la propriété.”

“L’acheteur est informé qu’un système de climatisation centrale est installé ou sera installé dans les logements qui permettra aux occupant(e)s de garder leurs fenêtres fermées si le bruit du trafic routier interfère avec leurs activités intérieures.”

2.2 Terrains touchés :

  • Tous les logements résidentiels des deux tours

Clause d’avertissement :

“Afin d’obtenir un environnement sonore intérieur convenable, les fenêtres pourraient devoir rester fermées, par conséquent, le logement est muni d’un système de climatisation centrale.”.

“Les acheteurs et les locataires sont informés que malgré l’inclusion de mesures de contrôle du bruit au sein de la présente zone de développement et des logements, le niveau de bruit occasionné par un trafic ferroviaire en hausse peut continuer de perturber à l’occasion certaines activités des occupant(e)s, car il peut excéder la norme ’s de la municipalité et du ministère de l’Environnement, de la Conservation et des Parcs.”

“Avertissement : La compagnie ferroviaire CN, ses ayants droit et ayants cause ont une emprise du chemin de fer à l’intérieur de 300 m du terrain des présentes. Il pourrait avoir des altérations ou des expansions des installations ferroviaires sur ladite emprise ultérieurement, incluant la possibilité qu’il y ait une compagnie ferroviaire en vertu d’un accord signé avec la présente compagnie ferroviaire pour utiliser l’emprise ou élargir leurs possibilités pour les ayants droit ou les ayants cause tiers comme susmentionné. L’expansion peut affecter le milieu de vie des résident(e)s du voisinage nonobstant ce qui précède, l’inclusion de toute mesure d’atténuation du bruit ou de la vibration dans la conception d’habitation individuelle ou multifamiliale. La compagnie ferroviaire CN ne sera pas tenue responsable de toute plainte ou réclamation découlant de l’utilisation des présentes installations ou de leur exploitation, concernant ou en vertu de l’emprise ferroviaire mentionnée plus haut.”

“Avertissement : La compagnie ferroviaire GO/ORBY, ses ayants droit et ayants cause ont une emprise du chemin de fer à l’intérieur de 300 m du terrain des présentes. Il pourrait avoir des altérations ou des expansions des installations ferroviaires sur ladite emprise ultérieurement, incluant la possibilité qu’il y ait une compagnie ferroviaire en vertu d’un accord signé avec la présente compagnie ferroviaire pour utiliser l’emprise ou élargir leurs possibilités pour les ayants droit ou les ayants cause tiers comme susmentionné. L’expansion peut affecter le milieu de vie des résident(e)s du voisinage nonobstant ce qui précède, l’inclusion de toute mesure d’atténuation du bruit ou de la vibration dans la conception d’habitation individuelle ou multifamiliale. La compagnie ferroviaire GO/ORBY ne sera pas tenue responsable de toute plainte ou réclamation découlant de l’utilisation des présentes installations ou de leur exploitation, concernant ou en vertu de l’emprise ferroviaire mentionnée plus haut.”

“Avertissement : Le développement se situe à proximité d’une intersection entre deux lignes de voie ferrée et est adjacent à une station GO Train. Les activités ferroviaires associées à la station et à l’aiguillage notamment, la circulation, le démarrage et l’arrêt de trains peuvent causer du bruit.”

“Les acheteurs et les locataires sont avisés que malgré l’inclusion de mesures d’atténuation du bruit au sein de ce développement et dans les logements, le niveau de bruit occasionné par le trafic ferroviaire en hausse continue d’être problématique compte tenu du niveau de bruit sur les balcons qui dépasse la norme du ministère de l’Environnement, de la Conservation et des Parcs” et compte tenu du respect des normes en matière de bruit du ministère pour les aires communes de séjour extérieures protégées au sein du développement.

en-US.Reports.ApplicantLandlordEmploymentRefer: Employer Reference (Ask for Human Resources/Payroll Supervisor)
Previous Landlord Reference (if current landlord is less than 2 years)
1. How long did the customer live there?
2. Did the customer give proper notice? If no, reason for moving out?
3. Was the rent paid on time? If no, when was rent paid by?
4. Was there any NSF? If yes, how many and were they cleared up?
5. Was there any noise complaints issued? If yes, how many?

en-US.Reports.CustomerMoveInWelcomeEmail:
Hello {0},

Welcome to {1}. We’re so happy you’ve chosen to call Boardwalk home.

As you get settled into your new place, we wanted to share some tips to help you take advantage of everything Boardwalk has to offer.

Create an account on our Resident Member Portal powered by Yuhu

This is a quick and easy way to find information about your lease, pay your rent, submit and track maintenance requests, book amenities, RSVP to community events and more!

Keep an eye out for an activation email to get started.

Check out our Resident Perks on bwalk.com

A life at Boardwalk comes with countless benefits. Check out www.bwalk.com/en-CA/Blog/Posts/Perks/ for our exclusive Resident Member offers designed to add value to your life.

Let us know how we did

We are dedicated to providing you with superior customer service. To assist us with this, please share your move-in experience by completing a short survey at bwalk.com/move-in. Please don’t hesitate to contact your On-site Customer Service Representative at {2} with any questions or comments regarding your new home.

Sincerely,

{3}

en-US.CommonTerms.WorkRequestContractorText:
Dear Contractor,

The following is a work request from Boardwalk Rental Communities. Please advise whether you are able to attend to do this work by either Accepting or Declining this request.
If accepted, a Purchase Order (authorization to begin work) will follow immediately. If declined, no expectation of your company completing this work will exist, and the job will be reassigned to another vendor.

Thank you.
Boardwalk Rental Communities
en-US.CommonTerms.InvestorCorporateGovernanceS:
The governance of our Trust is based on our guiding principles and the mandate of our Board of Trustees. These principles and values transcend through all Boardwalk Associates through their daily professional conduct. Boardwalk has clear policies in relation to corporate responsibility, including corporate governance and ethics, employee relations, audit and risk management.

Our Mission, Vision and Values as well as our Code of Conduct provide a framework for our Trustees and Associates when dealing with complex and sensitive issues that may arise in the normal course of business. These standards also serve to provide accountability to those who do not adhere to these values and standards.

As a Trust listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX), Boardwalk either meets or exceeds the guidelines set-out by the TSX regarding effective corporate governance. A detailed description of our corporate governance practices, as well as the disclosure required by the TSX, can be found in our Annual Information Form (AIF) filed on SEDAR in connection with our annual filing requirements.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.LabelSnowRemova: If the Apartment is a townhouse or other ground floor apartment with access to the exterior of the premises, the Tenant(s) agrees that they shall be responsible for removing any and/all snow on the walkway from the main sidewalk in front of the Building to their Apartment, including, but not limited to, the steps up to and any patio forming part of their Apartment.  The Tenant(s) further agrees that any and all snow shall be removed from the above noted areas in a timely manner after each snowfall, but, in any event, within 24 hours of each and every snowfall. The Tenant agrees to indemnify the Landlord for any losses, claims or damages arising from their failure to remove snow from the above noted areas as set forth in this paragraph, as well as for any breach of this paragraph {0}.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.LabelCompliance: {0}. COMPLIANCE WITH APPLICABLE LAWS: Tenant expressly covenants and agrees to comply with all applicable laws, rules and regulations when using and/or enjoying the Apartment, Building and/or any utilities supplied by the Landlord, including, but not limited to, the Copyright Act (Canada), as amended.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.LabelSeverabili: {0}. SEVERABILITY: If any covenant, obligation, or agreement of this Lease Agreement or the application thereof to any person or circumstance shall, to any extent, be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Lease Agreement or the application of such covenant, obligation, or agreement to persons or circumstances other than those as to which it is held invalid or unenforceable shall not be affected thereby, and each covenant, obligation, and agreement of this Agreement shall be separately valid and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law.
en-US.Reports.LeaseRenewalAndExpiryLetter.Sect:
We truly hope that you have enjoyed making {0} your home over the past year. We have certainly enjoyed having you be a part of our Community, but sadly our records show that your current lease expires on {1}.

We sincerely hope you will continue to make {0} your home for the next year and would like to invite you to meet with me personally, {2}, or our On-site Customer Service Representative, to discuss current market conditions and explore our current offers.
Though your current incentive expires with your current lease, we are confident, together, we can continue to provide you with exceptional value.

Renewing your lease now at competitive market pricing gives you the opportunity to lock in your monthly rental expense for a full year regardless of any increases due to changing market conditions. Should you decide to renew your lease, please ask about our Pre-Authorized Payment Program which can greatly simplify paying your monthly rent and parking.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.LabelSnowRemova: If the Apartment is a townhouse or other ground floor apartment with access to the exterior of the premises, the Tenant(s) agrees that they shall be responsible for removing any and/all snow on the walkway from the main sidewalk in front of the Building to their Apartment, including, but not limited to, the steps up to and any patio forming part of their Apartment.  The Tenant(s) further agrees that any and all snow shall be removed from the above noted areas in a timely manner after each snowfall, but, in any event, within 24 hours of each and every snowfall. The Tenant agrees to indemnify the Landlord for any losses, claims or damages arising from their failure to remove snow from the above noted areas as set forth in this paragraph, as well as for any breach of this paragraph {0}.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.LabelCannabis2: Notwithstanding R. v. Parker[2000] O.J. No. 2787; Allard v. Canada 2014 FC 280, or any other common law rule to the contrary, The Tenant(s) covenants and agrees that they shall not smoke, and/or cultivate or otherwise grow cannabis and/or marijuana (collectively, “Cannabis”) in the rental unit at any time. The Tenant further covenants and agrees that purchasing or selling of any amount of Cannabis anywhere in or on the rental complex is strictly prohibited, and as such, is a breach of this Lease and applicable law, including, but not limited to, residential tenancy laws in force in the province in which the rental complex is located. Other than in a non-smoking form in the rental unit, use of Cannabis anywhere in the rental complex, including, but not limited to, common areas, hallways, or in or on the balcony(s) and/or patio(s) of the rental unit and rental complex, is strictly prohibited and a breach of this Lease. The Tenant(s) is responsible to ensure that their use of non-smoking form of Cannabis does not create a nuisance and/or interfere with the reasonable enjoyment of the Landlord or other Tenants of the rental complex, does not cause damage to the rental unit or rental complex, and does not impair the safety of any persons in the rental unit or rental complex. The Tenant agrees to indemnify the Landlord for any losses, claims or damages arising from their use of Cannabis, as well as for any breach of this paragraph B.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1AtoK(V2.2:
C. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
D. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
E. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
F. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
G. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
H. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
I. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
J. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, or with the lights of the Building, which are not within the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
K. CHILDREN: Children are not allowed as residents without consent of the Landlord. Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
L. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1AtoK(V2.1:
A. ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION: Boardwalk conducts their business in a manner that protects our environment and complies with all applicable environmental laws. We believe in a proactive approach to protecting Resident Members, Associates and property from environmental hazards. As part of our commitment, we develop programs that promote conservation. We are dedicated to the responsible use of natural resources and the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions.
The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules to preserve and protect our environment:
a) COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
b) SMOKING: The Tenant(s) acknowledges that smoking is restricted to their suite or the balcony/patio affixed to their suite only.
c) PARKING: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately cleaning up engine oil leaks.
d) TOXIC OR LIKE GOODS: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately disposing of hazardous materials at designated municipality drop-off locations.
e) SINK DRAINS AND TOILETS: Grease is not to be poured down the sink drains or into toilets.

en-US.Reports.LeaseAgreement.AB.Section7(V2): 7. The Tenant(s) is responsible for all utilities not provided by the landlord and all utilities must be in the Tenant’s name at move in and remain so until the tenancy has ended, including water that is billed using a Ratio Utility Billing System (RUBS), which will be billed to the Tenant by the utility service provider as described in Schedule A. The Landlord agrees that the following are included with the rent:
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label8(V3):
{0}. UTILITIES: The Tenant(s) acknowledges that the supply of all utilities, including but not restricted to, cable television (wire line, satellite, and microwave), internet cable, telephone, natural gas, electric, heat and water, shall be provided by either the Landlord or suppliers selected by the Landlord and the Tenant(s) may not contract separately for such services without written consent of the Landlord, which consent may be withheld for any reason within the Landlord's discretion (this clause is limited to natural gas, electric, heat and water for Ontario).
In the event the Landlord selects a third party supplier to provide one or more utilities to a Tenant, the Tenant(s) shall be responsible for transferring the applicable utilities into their name.
In addition to the utility charge, the Tenant shall be responsible for and covenants to pay any administration, service or other fee charged by said third party supplier, as well as an administration fee of $25.00 will be charged for all bills processed by the Landlord on behalf of the Tenant(s) (collectively, ”Fees”).
The Tenant(s) also acknowledges that where a utility is currently included in the rent the Landlord, in its sole discretion, may, at any time, choose to meter the Tenant's rented premises separately and transfer responsibility for payment of the utility (and any related Fees) directly to the Tenant based on the Tenant's own consumption.
Any master metered utilities, (which currently includes water and sewer, but which in the future may include gas or electric), are based upon the bills received by the Landlord during the prior month. These bills will be totaled to determine the total Ratio Utility Billing System (“RUBS”) billing. RUBS calculates each Tenant’s portion of the total utility bills based on the total building(s) estimated leasable square footage and the approximate square footage of each apartment but removes utilities used in common areas and removes seasonal spikes for summer irrigation. RUBS billing allocates cost for utilities in a much less precise way than separate meters and may charge similar rates for Tenants with significantly different usage patterns and appliances. This system encourages all Tenants to report leaks and conserve utilities. Each Tenants monthly RUBS bill is calculated as follows:
      Monthly charge = (Size) x (Rate)
      Size = apartment square footage
      Rate = ([total bulk utility bills] - [common area usage] - [seasonal spike]) / building total leasable square footage

en-US.Reports.PAPNSFWarning.BodyV2:
Please be advised that your pre-authorized payment for {0} did not clear your account by the bank for the following reason:

Pre-Authorized Payment Returned. ({1})

Thank you for promptly resubmitting payment to the rental office.

A {2} administration fee for this returned payment has been charged to your account.

Please note, should three (3) payments not clear your account in a six (6) month period, you will be removed from the Pre-Authorized Payment program. Should this happen, all future payments must be in the form of debit payments at the leasing office or by Visa or Mastercard debit or credit card on our Resident Member Portal. Once payment has been received (in full) on or before the first day of each month for the next six (6) months, you may reapply for the Pre-Authorized Payment program.

Should you have any questions or concerns, please contact your Customer Service Representative at {3}.

Thank you for your tenancy with {4}.

Sincerely,

en-US.Reports.RentalIncreaseLetterQC.Negotiati: To help you negotiate a rent increase agreement, the Tribunal recommends using its tool
How to agree on the rent

and having invoices and accounts justifying the proposed increase. This tool is available from your local Tribunal office, and an interactive version is available on the Tribunal’s website(www.tal.gouv.qc.ca/en).
en-US.Reports.RentalIncreaseLetterQC.Applicati: Application to the Tribunal administratif du logement to fix the rent

If the lessee objects to the proposed modifications, such as a rent increase, the lessor may apply to the Tribunal administratif du logement, within one month after receiving the notice of objection, to have the rent fixed or for a ruling on any other modification of the lease. The lessee and the lessor must then comply with the decision of the Tribunal. If the lessor does not apply to the Tribunal within the month following the objection, the lease is renewed at the same rent and on the same other conditions.
The lessor’s notice and the lessee’s response must be given within the time frames in the following table.

en-US.Reports.AbandonmentNotice.SK.Body:
1. The Tenant(s) appear to have abandoned the Premises;
2. Rental arrears in the amount of {0} have not been paid; and,
3. The Landlord is concerned regarding the security and condition of the Premises;


TAKE NOTICE THAT THE LANDLORD, {1}, as a result of your abandonment of the Premises, hereby accepts your repudiation of the residential Lease Agreement.

The Landlord has changed the locks on the Premises in order to secure the Premises. Keys to the Premises are available to the tenant upon request. The Landlord can be contacted at {2}.

TAKE NOTICE THAT it is the landlord’s intention to dispose of any abandoned goods in accordance with Section 85(4.b) of the Residential Tenancies Act. If you have not abandoned the Premises, please call the Landlord within 48 hours. The Landlord will also be seeking against you, as Tenant, all damages, costs and expenses, including legal fees and disbursements, arising from your breach of the Lease Agreement.



Issue Date: {3} {4}, {5}.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1AtoK(V3):
A. ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION: Boardwalk conducts their business in a manner that protects our environment and complies with all applicable environmental laws. We believe in a proactive approach to protecting Resident Members, Associates and property from environmental hazards. As part of our commitment, we develop programs that promote conservation. We are dedicated to the responsible use of natural resources and the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions.
The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules to preserve and protect our environment:
a) COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
b) SMOKING: IS NOT PERMITTED IN THE RENTAL UNIT OR ON THE PROPERTY. The Tenant shall not, nor permit, any guest, employee, occupant, agent or invitee of the Tenant or any Occupants, to smoke any combustible products of any kind (including, without limitation, tobacco, cigarettes, marijuana or any other substance) in or on the Property or the Rental Unit, including but not limited to hallways, stairways, foyers, common rooms and facilities, decks, patios, exterior landings, front steps, entrance ways, fire escapes, basements, storage areas, parking areas, driveways, walkways, lawns, gardens, adjoining grounds, building facilities and any area within 7.5 meters of any doorway, air intake or window of the Property unless the Landlord specifically authorizes same in writing. For the purposes of this section, smoking includes vaporizing and the use of e-cigarettes or similar devices. This section is material to the Lease.
c) PARKING: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately cleaning up engine oil leaks.
d) TOXIC OR LIKE GOODS: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately disposing of hazardous materials at designated municipality drop-off locations.
e) SINK DRAINS AND TOILETS: Grease is not to be poured down the sink drains or into toilets.

en-US.CommonTerms.VeteransDiscountConfirmation:
Please ensure that you have seen one of the following forms of proof of service in the Canadian Military before applying the Veterans/Active Military Discount.

  • Record of Service Card – This is given to any military person who has been in the service for 10 years
  • Veteran Affairs Health ID Card – Given to military personal who have an open medical file with Veteran Affairs
  • Discharge Document – Certificate given to any military person who has served in the military.DND ID card for National Defense (NDI 20) – Canadian Forces Identification Card
  • CFOne Card – This is a service discount card Military Personnel can apply for
  • CF Health Care Identification Card – This is given to all full time Canadian Forces members and entitled persons

    • Have you seen one of the documents?

en-US.Reports.LeaseScheduleBRC.Label1to3(V2):
1. Notice of increase in rent
The Landlord shall not increase the rent payable under this Lease Agreement or recover any additional rent resulting from an increase unless the Landlord serves on the Tenant(s) a written notice of the increase in rent at least 90 days before the date on which the increase is to be effective.
2. Termination for substantial breach by Tenant(s)
If the Tenant(s) commits a substantial breach of this Lease Agreement, the Landlord may apply to a court to terminate the tenancy or may terminate the tenancy by serving the Tenant(s) with a notice at least 14 days before the day that the tenancy is to terminate.
3. Termination of tenancy for damage or assault
Notwithstanding section 2 above, if the Tenant(s) has:
(a) done or permitted significant damage to the Apartment, the common areas of the Building or the property of which they form a part, or
(b) physically assaulted or threatened to physically assault the Landlord or another tenant,
the Landlord may apply to a court to terminate the tenancy or may terminate the tenancy by serving the Tenant(s) with a notice at least 24 hours before the time that the tenancy is to terminate.

en-US.Reports.RentalIncreaseLetter.LetterBody1: At Boardwalk Rental Communities we aspire to bring you the best value in quality rental communities at competitive prices. Value is measured by the marketplace by comparing our rents vs. competitors with similar locations, amenities and services and by considering the cost of home ownership. We will continue to maintain your building to Boardwalk Rental Communities standards and invest in improvements as necessary. We take pride in maintaining your building to the highest standards and sincerely hope that you feel the same way that we do.

In substitution to the above noted rental increase, please note that, effective the same day as the rental increase, you may choose to have the gas in your suite sub-metered and invoiced separately by EnerCare Connections on a go-forward basis. If you choose this option, gas will no longer be included in your rent and you will be responsible for paying them separately to the Landlord on the same day your Rental Charge is due. If you choose this option, your rent increase will only be {0}. If you do not choose this option prior to the effective date of your rent increase, you will be deemed to accept the market rent amount and gas will continue to be included in your rent. en-US.Reports.CustomerNoticeToVacate.Label6(V4: applies to tenants on a month-to-month lease. en-US.Reports.ApologyNotice.Body:
We regret to inform you that we have accidentally deposited your postdated cheque of {0}. As such, your cheque was cancelled and returned to us by the bank without debit to your account. We apologize for any inconvenience that we may have caused. At this time, please resubmit another cheque to serve as replacement for the cancelled cheque that was returned.

Please contact your resident manager, or the management office for further details, and to have your cancelled cheque returned in exchange for a replacement.

Again, Please accept our apology, and our gratitude for your patience and understanding.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1: 1. RULES: The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules:
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.ABSS.Label1: {0}. RULES: The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules:
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.ABSS.Label1AtoP(V2:
A. ENVIROMENTAL PROTECTION: Boardwalk conducts their business in a manner that protects our environment and complies with all applicable environmental laws. We believe in a proactive approach to protecting Resident Members, Associates and property from environmental hazards. As part of our commitment, we develop programs that promote conservation. We are dedicated to the responsible use of natural resources and the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions.
The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules to preserve and protect our environment:
a) COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
b) SMOKING: The Tenant(s) acknowledges that smoking is restricted to suites only.
c) PARKING: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately cleaning up engine oil leaks.
d) TOXIC OR LIKE GOODS: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately disposing of hazardous materials at designated municipality drop-off locations.
e) SINK DRAINS AND TOILETS: Grease is not to be poured down the sink drains or into toilets.
B. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
C. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
D. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
E. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
F. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
G. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
H. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
I. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, or with the lights of the Building, which are not within the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
J. CHILDREN: Children are not allowed as residents without consent of the Landlord. Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
K. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
L. ANIMALS: Pets are not permitted.
M. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke detectors monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke detector. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
M. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke alarms monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke alarm. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
N. WIRING: No wires for electric lights, television, or radio connections or otherwise are to be introduced, nor the position of any existing wires altered without written consent from the Landlord.
O. GUESTS: Guests may not stay longer than 7 days without written consent from the Landlord. Guests may not park overnight on the property without permission from the Landlord; violators will be ticketed or towed. If handicapped parking is available, guests must display a valid handicapped placard. The Tenant(s) is responsible for the actions of their guests.
P. APPLIANCES: Appliances are leased as part of the Apartment. The Tenant(s) will keep them in clean condition.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.LabelCannabis1: B. CANNABIS/MARIJUANA CULTIVATION AND USE: Initialed by Tenant(s):
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.LabelCannabis2: Notwithstanding R. v. Parker[2000] O.J. No. 2787; Allard v. Canada 2014 FC 280, or any other common law rule to the contrary, The Tenant(s) covenants and agrees that they shall not smoke, and/or cultivate or otherwise grow cannabis and/or marijuana (collectively, “Cannabis”) in the rental unit at any time. The Tenant further covenants and agrees that purchasing or selling of any amount of Cannabis anywhere in or on the rental complex is strictly prohibited, and as such, is a breach of this Lease and applicable law, including, but not limited to, residential tenancy laws in force in the province in which the rental complex is located. Other than in a non-smoking form in the rental unit, use of Cannabis anywhere in the rental complex, including, but not limited to, common areas, hallways, or in or on the balcony(s) and/or patio(s) of the rental unit and rental complex, is strictly prohibited and a breach of this Lease. The Tenant(s) is responsible to ensure that their use of non-smoking form of Cannabis does not create a nuisance and/or interfere with the reasonable enjoyment of the Landlord or other Tenants of the rental complex, does not cause damage to the rental unit or rental complex, and does not impair the safety of any persons in the rental unit or rental complex. The Tenant agrees to indemnify the Landlord for any losses, claims or damages arising from their use of Cannabis, as well as for any breach of this paragraph B.

en-US.Reports.LeaseAgreement.BC.Section3: 3. SECURITY DEPOSIT: The Tenant(s) agrees to pay the Landlord a Security Deposit in the amount of {0}. The Tenant(s) agrees that interest on this deposit will be refunded at the end of tenancy. See Schedule B, paragraph 14.

Rent paid to the Landlord by someone other than the Tenant(s) shall be deemed to have been paid on behalf of the Tenant(s).
en-US.Reports.LeaseAgreement.BC.Section4Sub: (b) In addition to the rent set out in sub paragraph (a) the Tenant shall pay to the Landlord the cost of the {0} consumed by the Tenant during the term as follows:
en-US.Reports.LeaseAgreement.BC.Section4SubSub:
i) The charge will be at the same rate the Landlord pays to the main {0} supplier for the Building and the Tenant shall pay an administration fee determined by the Landlord, in its sole discretion.
ii) The charges in subparagraph i) above will be invoiced to the Tenant monthly, which shall be due and payable by the 1st day of the 2nd month following the date(s) of consumption. For example, payment for January consumption is due March 1.
iii) The Tenant shall receive as a credit any utility(s) subsidies the Landlord receives from any federal, provincial, or municipal government which are directly applicable to the utility(s) consumed by the Tenant.
iv) In the final month of the Lease, the Tenant’s utility account shall be presented for payment on the move out date, and shall be based on the rate for the previous month, and shall be due and payable by the Tenant within five days of receipt.

For illustration purposes only, the fees charged by the submetering provider engaged by the Landlord for the Building as of the date hereof are a {1} submetering fee, which are subject to an annual adjustment based on the consumer price index for the municipality in which the Building is located.

en-US.Reports.LeaseRenewalAndExpiryLetter.Sect:
Of course, we hope you will continue to call Boardwalk home and enjoy the many benefits of living with us such as a friendly and knowledgeable on-site service team, 24-hour customer service, Resident Member events, and Boardwalk’s high standard of cleanliness and comfort. Our communities are enriched by Resident Members like you who take pride in where they live. We know you have choices and we truly hope you will continue to choose {0} as your home.

In the event you’re letting your lease come to term and vacating by the end of the month, we would like for you to keep in mind the following. If you are currently signed up for our Pre-authorized Payment Program, you will need to provide a cancellation to your leasing office prior to the 23rd of the month in which you’re vacating so no future withdraws will occur. If you have already provided a Notice to vacate to the leasing office prior to the 23rd this will be done automatically.

Again, we wish to take this opportunity to thank you for being a part of the {0} Community and should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at {1}.
en-US.Labels.ArrearsNoticeCoverLetter-V1E: In hopes of avoiding legal action (which can result in additional costs to you), we ask you to please contact us immediately. We always strive to work with our Resident Members on mutual resolves and understand how some financial difficulties arise outside of our control. We look forward to working with you to resolve this situation.
en-US.Reports.LeaseRenewalAndExpiryLetter.Sect: If you are planning to continue to make {0} your home for the next year, please contact your On-site Customer Service Representative and ask about our current offers.

As a valued Resident Member, we would like to offer you a lease renewal of {0} per month for a 12-month term. If you are planning to continue to make {1} your home for the next year, we encourage you to take advantage of this rental rate.
Renewing your lease now at current market rent gives you the opportunity to lock in your rental expense for a full year regardless of any rental increase due to changing market conditions.

en-US.Reports.LeaseRenewalAndExpiryLetter.Sect: {0} has installed {1} sub-meters for every suite.  This allows Resident Member(s) to control their own {1} consumption.  Currently, Resident Member(s) who consciously conserve their {1} consumption do not see any benefit for their actions.  With the {1} sub-meters, your conservation efforts will be reflected in your utility bills, as you are no longer subsidizing other consumers.  In addition, your conservation efforts will have a dramatic beneficial environmental impact on our community.

Please be advised that if you do sign a new lease your suite will be sub-metered for your consumption. Also, any rental increases you have received that have not yet started will be rescinded and the new lease will take precedence.

We would also like to inform you that your internal subsidy will expire at the same time as your lease. If you would like your subsidy to  continue you must re-apply by completing the application available from your Customer Service Representative. In order to ensure your application is processed before your lease expires it must be received along with all required documentation at least two weeks prior to expiry.

We hope that you will continue to enjoy the many benefits of living in a Boardwalk property as we strive to maintain your building to a high standard of cleanliness and comfort.  Amenities such as a friendly and knowledgeable service team and 24-hour customer service are a few features that will allow you to continue to enjoy living in a Boardwalk community.

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme: PART II

Schedule 1
[Section 4]
Standard Conditions of a Tenancy Agreement
The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006

NOTE: These Standard Conditions are conditions of every tenancy agreement. However, both landlord and tenant should consult The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 (the “Act”) and The Residential Tenancies Regulations, 2007 (the “regulations”) to determine the full extent of their rights and obligations. If there is a conflict between a provision in these Standard Conditions and a provision in the Act or the regulations, the provision in the Act or regulations prevails.
Application of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006
Written tenancy agreements

1(1) These standard conditions form part of every tenancy agreement.
(2) The terms and conditions of any tenancy agreement, and any changes or additions to the terms and conditions, may not contradict or change any right, obligation or standard condition under The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 or a regulation made pursuant to that Act.
(3) If a term or condition of a tenancy agreement does contradict or change such a right, obligation or standard condition, the term or condition of the tenancy agreement is void and cannot be enforced.

2(1) Tenancy agreements do not have to be in writing. However, if a landlord and tenant enter into a written agreement, it must comply with the provisions of the Act and the regulations. The landlord must give the tenant a copy of the signed agreement within 20 days after the agreement is entered into.
(2) A fixed term tenancy for more than three months must be in writing, failing which it will be deemed to be a monthly tenancy. In addition, any written tenancy agreement that does not set out the date on which it is to end will also be deemed to be a monthly tenancy.
(3) Even if a tenancy agreement is not in writing, the landlord must provide the tenant with an address for service and telephone number as well as a telephone number for emergency repairs.
[see sections 19, 20 and 21 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
7 A tenant is entitled to quiet enjoyment of a rental unit. This includes a right to:

3(1) A security deposit may not exceed the equivalent of one month’s rent. A tenant does not have to pay more than 50% of the security deposit on the date the tenancy agreement is entered into. The balance of the security deposit must be paid within two months after the tenant takes possession of the rental unit. (Special rules apply if the Minister responsible for The Saskatchewan Assistance Act guarantees payment of the security deposit.)
(2) If a landlord accepts a security deposit that is greater than one month’s rent, the tenant may deduct the overpayment from rent or apply to the Director of Residential Tenancies to recover the overpayment.
[see sections 25 and 26 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Landlord’s notice at end of employment with the landlord

4(1) A tenant must pay rent when it is due, whether or not his or her landlord complies with all requirements of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006. If a landlord is in breach of the Act or the regulations, a tenant should, instead of withholding rent, make an application to the Director of Residential Tenancies.
(2) A landlord must provide a tenant with a receipt for rent paid in cash.
(3) A landlord must not terminate or restrict a service or facility without the tenant’s consent unless the landlord obtains an order from the Director of Residential Tenancies.
(4) A landlord is prohibited from imposing charges or increasing rent for a service or facility that was previously available at no cost, unless the tenant agrees or the landlord obtains an order from the Director of Residential Tenancies.
(5) A tenancy agreement must not include a provision that all or part of the rent payable for the remainder of the term of the tenancy agreement becomes due and payable if the tenant breaches a provision of the tenancy agreement.
[see sections 41, 42 and 43 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Rent increase

5(1) Subject to subsection (1.1), a landlord must give a tenant written notice of a rent increase for a periodic tenancy at least six months before the effective date of the increase.
(2) If a landlord fails to give the required notice, the rent increase won’t take effect until six months after it was given. If a landlord increases rent without proper notice, the tenant can make an application to the Director of Residential Tenancies for compensation.
[see section 54 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Assignment and subletting
Protection of tenant’s right to quiet enjoyment
[see section 44 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme:
6(1) If a tenant has entered into a tenancy agreement for a fixed period (as opposed to a “month to month” tenancy), a tenant may sublet a rental unit only with the written consent of the landlord. However, the landlord must not unreasonably withhold consent to the proposed sublease and must not charge a tenant a fee of more than $20 for considering or consenting to a sublease.
(2) If a rental unit has been sublet, the original tenant remains responsible for fulfilling the tenant’s obligations under The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 and the tenancy agreement with respect to matters that arose before the date the unit was sublet.
[see section 50 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 and section 7 of The Residential Tenancies Regulations, 2007]

(a) reasonable privacy;
(b) freedom from unreasonable disturbance from the landlord or other tenants;
(c) exclusive possession of the rental unit; and
(d) use of common areas for reasonable and lawful purposes.

(2) If a landlord does not have permission from the tenant and there is no written agreement, the landlord must make a reasonable effort, at least two hours before entry, to contact the tenant at a phone number or e-mail address provided by the tenant. If the landlord is still unsuccessful in notifying the tenant, or the tenant has not provided contact information, the landlord may enter the unit without prior notice by posting a notice on the door of the rental unit that sets out the time and date of entry.
[see section 7 of The Residential Tenancies Regulations, 2006]

8(1) A landlord must maintain rental property in a good state of repair and fit for the use and enjoyment of the tenant. A landlord must also keep all services and facilities included with the rent (e.g., appliances, heating and plumbing systems, etc.) in a good and functional state of repair.
(2) A tenant must maintain reasonable health, cleanliness and sanitary standards throughout the rental unit and must repair damage to the rental unit, services or facilities caused by the tenant or someone permitted on the property by the tenant. However, the tenant is not responsible for reasonable wear and tear.
(3) If the landlord grants the tenant the exclusive use of residential property (such as a single family dwelling), the tenant is responsible for the ordinary cleanliness of the exterior of the property, including the yard or surrounding land, unless the parties agree otherwise.
[see section 49 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Landlord’s right to enter rental unit
9(1) A landlord must not enter a rental unit unless one of the following applies:

(a) the tenant gives permission at the time of the entry;
(b) at least 24 hours (and not more than seven days) before the entry the landlord gives the tenant written notice that sets out the date and time of entry and a reasonable purpose for entering;
(c) the landlord enters the unit to provide housekeeping or related services pursuant to a written agreement with the tenant;
(d) the landlord has an order from the Director of Residential Tenancies that authorizes the entry;
(e) the tenant appears to have abandoned the rental unit;
(f) an emergency exists and the entry is necessary to protect life or property.

(2) The notice provided by the landlord must state a maximum four-hour period during which the landlord will enter the rental unit. Entry can only be made between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m. on a day that is not a Sunday or a day of religious worship for the tenant, unless the tenant otherwise agrees.
(3) If a tenant has given notice to end the tenancy, the landlord may enter the rental unit for the purpose of showing it to prospective tenants, but only if the landlord complies with section 10 of these standard conditions.
(4) A landlord must not enter a rental unit for the purpose of showing it to a prospective purchaser without first giving the tenant 24 hours’ notice or obtaining the consent of the tenant.
[see section 45 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
10(1) If a tenant has given notice to end the tenancy, the landlord may enter the rental unit for the purpose of showing it to prospective tenants, but only if:

(a) the tenant has given permission;
(b) the landlord gives notice (which the tenant has received) at least two hours before entry; or
(c) the landlord and the tenant have agreed in writing to the circumstances under which the landlord may enter the rental unit, provided that the terms are reasonable and the agreement is entered into after the tenant has given notice to end the tenancy.
Landlord’s application for order ending tenancy early
Tenant’s right of access protected
11 A landlord must not restrict access to residential property (i.e., the rental unit and any common areas) by the tenant or a person permitted on the residential property by the tenant.
Prohibitions on changes to locks and other access

12(1) A landlord must not change locks or security codes to a rental unit unless the tenant agrees to the change and the landlord provides the tenant with new keys or new security codes for the unit. Similarly, a tenant must not change locks or security codes to a rental unit unless the landlord consents to the change or the Director of Residential Tenancies has ordered the change.
(2) A landlord must not change locks or security codes to a common area unless the landlord provides each tenant with new keys or new security codes for the area. Similarly, a tenant must not change locks or security codes to a common area unless the landlord consents to the change.
[see section 48 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
How a tenancy ends
13(1) A tenancy can be ended only if:
Tenant’s notice
Landlord’s notice for non-payment of rent
Landlord’s notice for cause

(a) the tenant or landlord gives written notice to end the tenancy in accordance with sections 56 to 61 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006; [see Standard Conditions 14 to 18, below]
(b) the landlord and tenant agree in writing to end the tenancy;
(c) the tenant vacates or abandons the rental unit;
(d) the tenancy agreement cannot continue due to causes outside the control of the landlord or tenant (e.g., a fire renders the premises uninhabitable);
(e) the Director of Residential Tenancies orders that the tenancy is ended.

(2) A tenancy for a fixed period (as opposed to a “month to month” tenancy) ends on the date specified in the tenancy agreement unless the landlord and tenant have entered into a new tenancy agreement.
[see section 55 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

15(1) A landlord may end a tenancy immediately by serving a notice to end the tenancy if rent is unpaid for a period of more than 15 days after it is due. If a tenant does not vacate the unit in response to the notice, the landlord can make an application to the Director of Residential Tenancies for possession of the unit.
(2) If the tenant is responsible for the payment of utilities and any utility charges are unpaid, the landlord may treat the unpaid utility charges as unpaid rent if the tenant fails to make payment within 15 days after a landlord has requested that the tenant make payment.
[see section 57 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

14(1) A tenant may end a month to month tenancy by giving the landlord notice at least one month before the day of the month on which rent is payable.
(2) A tenant may end a week to week tenancy by giving the landlord notice at least one week before the day of the week on which rent is payable.
(3) A tenant may end a tenancy on one day’s notice if the landlord is in breach of a “material” term of the agreement (e.g., the rental unit has become uninhabitable). However, if the breach of the agreement is capable of being remedied, the tenant must give the landlord a reasonable period to remedy the problem before ending the tenancy.
[see section 56 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
16(1) A landlord may end a tenancy by giving notice to end the tenancy if one or more of the following applies:

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme:
(a) the tenant does not pay the security deposit within 30 days after the date the payment is due;
(b) the tenant is repeatedly late paying rent;
(c) there are an unreasonable number of people living in a rental unit;
(d) the tenant (or a person permitted on the residential property by the tenant) has significantly interfered with or unreasonably disturbed other tenants or neighbours, has seriously jeopardized the health, safety or lawful rights of another tenant or neighbour, or has put the landlord’s property at significant risk;
(e) the tenant (or a person permitted on the residential property by the tenant) has engaged in noxious, offensive or illegal activity;
(f) the tenant does not repair damage to the residential property within a reasonable time;
(g) the tenant has breached an important term of the agreement and not remedied the problem within a reasonable time;
(h) the tenant attempts to sublet the rental unit without obtaining the landlord’s written consent;
(i) the tenant knowingly gives false information about the residential property to a prospective tenant or purchaser viewing the residential property;
(j) the rental unit must be vacated in accordance with the order of any lawful authority, including the Director of Residential Tenancies;
(k) the tenant (or a person permitted in the rental unit by the tenant) after receiving notice, continues to smoke in a house that is also the landlord’s principal residence.

(2) A notice to end the tenancy on any of the above grounds must be given in writing, no later than one month before the day of the month, (or week, in a weekly tenancy) that rent is payable under the tenancy agreement. The landlord must give the tenant a reasonable period of time to remedy the circumstances on which the notice is based, if they are capable of being remedied. A tenant may dispute a notice by giving written notice to the landlord within 15 days after receiving the notice, failing which, the tenant will be expected to vacate the unit by the date specified in the notice.
[see section 58 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme:
17(1) A landlord may apply to the Director of Residential Tenancies for an order that will end the tenancy early if it would be unreasonable to require the landlord to give notice under standard condition
(2) This type of order can become effective immediately and may be made if a tenant has done any of the following:

(a) significantly interfered with or unreasonably disturbed another tenant or another occupant of the residential property, the landlord or any persons in any adjacent property;
(b) seriously jeopardized the health or safety or a lawful right or interest of another tenant or another occupant of the residential property, the landlord or any persons in any adjacent property;
(c) put the landlord’s property at significant risk;
(d) engaged in a noxious, offensive or illegal activity that:

(i) has caused or is likely to cause damage to the landlord’s property;
(ii) has adversely affected or is likely to adversely affect the quiet enjoyment, security, safety or physical well-being of another tenant or another occupant of the residential property, the landlord or any persons in any adjacent property; or
(iii) has jeopardized or is likely to jeopardize a lawful right or interest of another tenant or another occupant of the residential property, the landlord or any persons in any adjacent property;
(e) caused extraordinary damage to the residential property.
[see section 68 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme:
18 A landlord may end the tenancy of his or her own employee after the employment is ended by providing at least one month’s notice.
[see section 59 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Landlord’s use of property

19 A landlord may, under certain circumstances, end a tenancy for reasons related to the landlord’s use of the property (e.g., demolition of the property, sale of the property to someone who will occupy the property, or use by a close family member or friend).
[The provisions in this regard are quite detailed and are set out in sections 60, 61 and 62 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.]
Leaving the rental unit at the end of a tenancy
20 When a tenant vacates a rental unit:

(a) the tenant must return all keys to the landlord; and
(b) the rental unit must be reasonably clean and undamaged, except for reasonable wear and tear.
[see section 51 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
When landlord may regain possession of rental unit
21 A landlord may not regain possession of a rental unit unless:

en-US.Reports.StandardConditionsTenancyAgreeme:
(a) the tenant has vacated or abandoned the rental unit; or
(b) the landlord obtains an order for possession, and a writ of possession has been directed to a sheriff, pursuant to subsection 70(13) of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.
[see also section 65 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Liability for not complying with this Act or a tenancy agreement

22 If a landlord or tenant does not comply with The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006, the regulations made pursuant to that Act or their tenancy agreement, the non-complying landlord or tenant must compensate the other for any resulting damage or loss, including loss of rent paid or payable. However, a landlord or tenant who claims compensation for any damage or loss must do whatever is reasonable to minimize the damage or loss.
[see section 8 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]
Public housing authorities
23(1) The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 and the regulations made pursuant to that Act contain special provisions regarding:
Notices
Disputes

Historical Note: 16 Feb 2007 cR-22.0001 Reg 1
Appendix, Part II.

(a) housing provided by public housing authorities; and
(b) living accommodation provided pursuant to a social housing program.

(2) The provisions contain different rules for security deposits, rent increases and termination for such tenancies.
[See Division 2 of Part III, Part IV and Part V of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

24 Notices required by The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 or The Residential Tenancies Regulations, 2007 must be in writing. Most notices and documents can be served by personal service, registered mail or ordinary mail. Individuals serving notices or documents should refer to section 82 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 for details.
[see section 82 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 and section 12 of The Residential Tenancies Regulations, 2007]

25(1) Either the tenant or the landlord has the right to make an application regarding a residential tenancy dispute as provided in The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.
(2) Any application regarding a residential tenancy dispute shall be made to the Director of Residential Tenancies in accordance with The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 and the regulations made pursuant to that Act.
[see section 70 of The Residential Tenancies Act, 2006]

en-US.Reports.TenantFireSafety.Title: Tenant Fire Safety Information
en-US.Reports.TenantFireSafety.Section1: What to do in case of a fire in your suite:
en-US.Reports.WaterSeepageLetter.Section4: Please ensure that you have tenant insurance (as per your signed lease agreement) to cover any loss of your personal belongings as Boardwalk will not be held liable.  You will also want to make sure that you clean up any seepage promptly so that your personal belongings are not damaged.
en-US.Reports.TenantFireSafety.Opening: Please ensure that all smoke detectors in your suite are tested monthly.  If you encounter a problem or if the detector is not working, please notify site staff immediately and the problem will be rectified.
en-US.Reports.TenantFireSafety.Section1Sub:
• Alert everyone in your suite as quickly as possible.
  • Leave your suite. Close the door behind you.
  • If the fire is in its very early stages, it is SAFE and you are able to do so, consider using a fire extinguisher to control the fire.
  • Sound the fire alarm system.
  • Walk, do not run, to the nearest exit and make your way out of the building.
  • Call the Fire Department (911) from a safe location.
  • Once you have left the fire area, do not return to it.
  • Inform site staff or Fire Department members of the fire location and any other important information.

en-US.Reports.TenantFireSafety.Section2Sub:
• When you hear the fire alarm, stop what you are doing.
  • Open suite door slowly and check corridor for conditions of smoke and fire.
  • Leave the building using the safest available exit.
  • If you are unable to leave, keep the suite door closed. Use a wet material to seal the door from smoke, wait by balcony or window and signal the attention of those outside. Do not panic.

In accordance with the Residential Tenancy Act, the Tenant(s) must provide 30 days written notice to terminate the tenancy where the termination date specified on the Notice to Vacate cannot be a date earlier than the last day of the lease term specified in this Lease Agreement. en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1AtoK(V1):
A. COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
B. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
C. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
D. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
E. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
F. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
G. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
H. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
I. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, or with the lights of the Building, which are not within the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
J. CHILDREN: Children are not allowed as residents without consent of the Landlord. Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
K. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1M(V1): N. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke detectors monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke detector. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1M(V2): N. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke alarms monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke alarm. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.

P. GUESTS: Guests may not stay longer than 7 days without written consent from the Landlord. Guests may not park overnight on the property without permission from the Landlord; violators will be ticketed or towed. If handicapped parking is available, guests must display a valid handicapped placard. The Tenant(s) is responsible for the actions of their guests.

ATTACHED DEVICES: Unless the Tenant(s) has the written consent of the Landlord, the Tenant(s) shall not attach, install or place any apparatus that receive broadcast signals from satellite or microwave on or about the Apartment, Building, balcony or property and without restricting the generality of the foregoing, shall not install, attach or place a satellite dish on or to the exterior walls of the Building, Apartment, balcony or property. en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1W(V1): X. PEST CONTROL: The tenant shall keep the rented premises free from vermin and in so doing shall procure and pay for any professional pest control service which may be necessary from time to time and a failure by the tenant to comply with this provision shall be deemed to constitute a consent that the landlord may enter the premises for the purpose of exterminating any such vermin and any costs thereof shall be payable to the landlord by the tenant. The tenant hereby further consents to entry of the landlord or an agent of the landlord for the purpose of treating the rented premises for pest control purposes.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label1W(V2): X. PEST CONTROL: The Tenant(s) represents and warrants that they have not experienced any pest issues or infestations during any prior tenancy(s) or, in the alternative, if they have experienced pest issues or infestations during a prior tenancy(s), their last treatment was on ____________________, 20_____. Prior to moving into the premises, the Tenant(s) will ensure that their belongings and the containers, luggage and/or boxes they are packed in do not contain any pest(s), including, but not limited to, bedbugs or cockroaches. The Tenant(s) will notify the Landlord immediately of any pest issues found in the rental premise. The Tenant(s) will not bring in any abandoned items found as these items may be infested with pests. The Landlord will cover the cost of pest treatment unless the Landlord determines, in its sole and unfettered discretion, that the pest(s) in question were introduced to the premises by the Tenant(s), in which case, at the option of the Landlord, in its sole and unfettered discretion, the cost of all required pest treatment(s) shall be borne by and for the account of the Tenant(s). The Tenant(s) must prepare their suite as per the pest control guidelines given and as directed by the Landlord, prior to the treatment date. The Tenant(s) will allow entry to the Landlord and the Landlord’s contractor for the purpose of taking the necessary steps to control or treat pests, as well as any inspections required for effective pest control and treatments. The Tenant(s) acknowledges and understands that it may require more than one treatment as a part of effective treatment of pests. The Tenant(s) understands that even if no pests are present in the rental premise, it may be deemed necessary to treat the rental premise anyway as a measure of pest prevention. Failure to comply with these terms will be considered a substantial breach of this lease agreement, and may result in charges and/or immediate eviction. The Tenant(s) shall indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord against all losses, damages and/or costs, including, but not limited to, solicitor and own client costs and charges, including paralegal and civil enforcement charges, incurred in enforcing this clause, for any breach by the Tenant(s) of this clause and/or any introduction of pest(s) to the premises by the Tenant(s).

In the event that the Tenant(s) breaches the Lease Agreement by vacating the premises prior to the end of the fixed term portion of the Lease Agreement, the Tenant(s) shall pay to the Landlord the sum of:

(a)$300.00; and

(b) any costs pre paid by the Landlord for utilities including, but not limited to, cable television (wire line, satellite, and microwave), internet cable and/or telephone
as liquidated damages which the parties agree is a genuine pre-estimate of damages for such breach. The payment of this amount shall not affect any of the other obligations of the Tenant(s) hereunder and any liability for any damage to the Apartment. If the Tenant(s) vacates the premises herein described, prior to the expiration of the term of this Lease Agreement, or if late notice to vacate is provided, any and all money received by the Tenant(s) as a move-in-allowance, and/or rental bonus, and/or rental incentives shall be immediately due and payable to the Landlord. In accordance with the Residential Tenancy Act, the Tenant(s) must provide 30 days written notice to terminate the tenancy where the termination date specified on the Notice to Vacate cannot be a date earlier than the last day of the lease term specified in this Lease Agreement. Any notice given after midnight on the first day of the month to vacate on the last day of the month shall be considered late notice and, as a result, the tenant(s) will be responsible for next month’s rent unless the suite is re-rented prior to this date.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label15Sub:
The word "information" means the Tenant's personal, business and credit information. It includes information provided to the Landlord by the Tenant(s), including through the products and services the Tenant(s) uses, and information obtained from others.
In order for Boardwalk to comply with federal and/or provincial privacy legislation, I/we understand that all personal Information collected from me/us may be collected, used and disclosed by Boardwalk for the purpose of my/our application assessment, for the purpose of debt collection, to uphold and maintain the rules and regulations of the property, to evaluate my/our tenancy, to comply with applicable law and in the ordinary course of Boardwalk's business, including, but not limited to, any refinancing or potential sale of the property, and normal course dealings with providers of utilities and other like services.
For greater certainty, and without limiting the generality of the foregoing paragraph, the Tenant(s) agrees to the use of its information as follows:
i. Use of Information - The Landlord may use the Information to establish and serve the Tenant(s) as its customer, determine whether any products or services of the Landlord are suitable for the Tenant(s), or when required or permitted by law. The Landlord may share information with its third party providers, including, but not limited to, providers of utilities, lenders and potential purchasers of its assets; and
ii. Collection and Use of Information - THE LANDLORD MAY OBTAIN INFORMATION FROM THIRD PARTIES, INCLUDING THROUGH A CREDIT CHECK, AND VERIFY INFORMATION WITH THEM. THE TENANT(S) AUTHORIZES THOSE PARTIES TO GIVE THE LANDLORD INFORMATION.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.DefineAct: The parties understand that where in this Tenancy Agreement, the words “the Act” are used, they refer to the Residential Tenancy Act, SBC2002, c.78 as amended and Regulation made form time to time.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1K: B. CANNABIS/MARIJUANA CULTIVATION AND USE: Initialed by Tenant(s):
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1KSub: The Tenant(s) covenants and agrees that they shall not smoke, and/or cultivate or otherwise grow cannabis and/or marijuana (collectively, “Cannabis”) in the Apartment at any time. The Tenant further covenants and agrees that purchasing or selling of any amount of Cannabis anywhere in or on the complex is strictly prohibited, and as such, is a breach of this Lease and applicable law. Other than in a non-smoking form in the Apartment, use of Cannabis anywhere in the complex, including, but not limited to, common areas, hallways, or in or on the balcony(s) and/or patio(s) of the Apartment and complex, is strictly prohibited and a breach of this Lease. The Tenant(s) is responsible to ensure that their use of non-smoking form of Cannabis does not create a nuisance and/or interfere with the reasonable enjoyment of the Landlord or other Tenants of the complex, does not cause damage to the Apartment or complex, and does not impair the safety of any persons in the Apartment or complex. The Tenant agrees to indemnify the Landlord for any losses, claims or damages arising from their use of Cannabis, as well as for any breach of this paragraph B.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1L(V1): C. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1AtoJ(V1):
The Tenant(s) agrees to abide by the following rules to preserve and protect our environment:
     a) COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
     b) SMOKING IS NOT PERMITTED IN THE RENTAL UNIT OR ON THE PROPERTY. The Tenant shall not, nor permit, any guest, employee, occupant, agent or invitee of the Tenant or any Occupants, to smoke any combustible products of any kind (including, without limitation, tobacco, cigarettes, marijuana or any other substance) in or on the Property or the Rental Unit, including but not limited to hallways, stairways, foyers, common rooms and facilities, decks, patios, exterior landings, front steps, entrance ways, fire escapes, basements, storage areas, parking areas, driveways, walkways, lawns, gardens, adjoining grounds, building facilities and any area within 7.5 meters of any doorway, air intake or window of the Property unless the Landlord specifically authorizes same in writing, or unless same is required to accommodate a person under the provisions of the British Columbia Human Rights Code. For the purposes of this section, smoking includes vaporizing and the use of e-cigarettes or similar devices. This section is material to the Lease.
     c) PARKING: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately cleaning up engine oil leaks.
     d) TOXIC OR LIKE GOODS: Tenant(s) is responsible for immediately disposing of hazardous materials at designated municipality drop-off locations.
     e) SINK DRAINS AND TOILETS: Grease is not to be poured down the sink drains or into toilets.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1L(V2):
D. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
E. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
F. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
G. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
H. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
I. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure which the Tenant(s) will be responsible for damages.
J. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, or with the lights of the Building, which are not within the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
K. CHILDREN: Children are not permitted to disturb other tenants by playing in common areas.
L. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1Q(V1): N. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke alarms monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke alarm. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
en-US.ValidationMessages.CannotPostIntoClosedP: Time cannot be posted into previous month because it is past the cut off period
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1Q(V2): O. WIRING: No wires for electric lights, television, or radio connections or otherwise are to be introduced, nor the position of any existing wires altered without written consent from the Landlord.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label1Q(V3):
P. GUESTS: Guests may not stay longer than 7 days without written consent from the Landlord. Guests may not park overnight on the property without permission from the Landlord; violators will be ticketed or towed.
