{0}. LOCKS: The Landlord must not change locks or other means of access to residential property unless the Landlord provides each Tenant with new keys or other means of access to the residential property. The Landlord must not change locks or other means of access to a rental unit unless the Tenant agrees and is given new keys. The Tenant must not change locks or other means of access to common areas of residential property, unless the Landlord consents to the change, or his or her rental unit, unless the Landlord consents to, or an arbitrator has ordered, the change.
{1}. REPAIRS:
Landlord's obligations: The Landlord must provide and maintain the residential property in a reasonable state of decoration and repair, suitable for occupation by a Tenant. The Landlord must comply with health, safety and housing standards required by law. If the Landlord is required to make a repair to comply with the above obligations, the Tenant may discuss it with the Landlord. If the Landlord refuses to make the repair, the Tenant may seek an arbitrator's order under the Act for the completion and costs of the repair.
Tenant's obligations: The Tenant must maintain reasonable health, cleanliness and sanitary standards throughout the rental unit and the other residential property to which the Tenant has access. The Tenant must take the necessary steps to repair damage to the residential property caused by the actions or neglect of the Tenant or a person permitted on the residential property by that Tenant. The Tenant is not responsible for repairs for reasonable wear and tear to the residential property. If the Tenant does not comply with the above obligations within a reasonable time, the Landlord may discuss the matter with the Tenant and may seek a monetary order through arbitration under the Act for the cost of repairs, serve a notice to end a tenancy, or both.
Emergency repairs: The Landlord must post and maintain in a conspicuous place on the residential property, or give to the Tenant in writing, the name and telephone number of the designated contact person for emergency repairs. If emergency repairs are required, the Tenant must make at least two attempts to telephone the designated contact person, and then give the Landlord reasonable time to complete the repairs. If the emergency repairs are still required, the Tenant may undertake the repairs, and claim reimbursement from the Landlord, provided a statement of account and receipts are given to the Landlord. If the Landlord does not reimburse the Tenant as required, the Tenant may deduct the cost from rent. The Landlord may take over completion of the emergency repairs at any time. Emergency repairs must be urgent and necessary for the health and safety of persons or preservation or use of the residential property and are limited to repairing major leaks in pipes or the roof, damaged or blocked water or sewer pipes or plumbing fixtures, the primary heating system, damaged or defective locks that give access to a rental unit, or the electrical systems.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label12(V1): {0}. MAINTENANCE REQUESTS: The Tenant(s), by requesting a maintenance work order, hereby permits the Landlord to enter the Apartment without notice within 72 hours to handle the request. Tenant(s) shall report all maintenance problems to the Landlord.

14. SECURITY DEPOSIT: Security Deposit and interest, if applicable, shall be returned at the end of the tenancy. Any Security Deposit refundable (including interest) will be paid to all current Tenants who have signed the Lease. The Tenant(s) shall leave a forwarding address in writing with the Landlord. The Tenant(s) will be notified of two scheduled move out dates and times. It is the Tenant(s) responsibility to be present at the move out inspection. If the Tenant(s) is not present at the first move out inspection, the Landlord will proceed with the second scheduled move out inspection. The Security Deposit may be utilized by the Landlord as necessary to provide for (but not limited to):
     a) Repairing any damage or loss to the Apartment, Building or grounds and their contents caused by the Tenant(s) or their guests. (Burns and other marks on carpets, flooring, furniture, appliances and walls shall not be considered normal wear and tear.)
     b) Any cleaning and damages to the Apartment that are indicated on the move out inspection report. Dirt is not considered normal wear and tear.
     c) Professional carpet cleaning. If carpet is cleaned by the Landlord, the Tenant(s) will be charged accordingly.
     d) The Tenant(s) is responsible for the amount of any damages or cleaning costs in excess of the Security Deposit, any outstanding rent or any other obligations of the Tenant(s).
15. OTHER RULES: Tenant(s) will obey any rules posted regarding the use and care of the Building, parking lot, laundry room and other facilities such as swimming pool, playground, etc. that are provided for the use of the Tenant(s). The Landlord may from time to time make such other and further reasonable rules for the care and cleanliness of the Building and grounds and for the comfort and convenience of the Tenant(s). Guests shall obey such rules.
16. SEVERABILITY: If any covenant, obligation, or agreement of this Lease or the application thereof to any person or circumstance shall, to any extent, be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this Lease or the application of such covenant, obligation, or agreement to persons or circumstances other than those as to which it is held invalid or unenforceable shall not be affected thereby, and each covenant, obligation, and agreement of this Agreement shall be separately valid and enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law.

A. COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s).
B. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
C. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
D. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
E. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
F. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.

H. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
I. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, or with the lights of the Building, which are not within the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
J. CHILDREN: Children are not allowed as residents without consent of the Landlord. Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
K. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
L. ANIMALS: Pets are not permitted.
M. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke detectors monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke detector. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.ABSS.Label1W(V2): W. PEST CONTROL: The Tenant(s) represents and warrants that they have not experienced any pest issues or infestations during any prior tenancy(s) or, in the alternative, if they have experienced pest issues or infestations during a prior tenancy(s), their last treatment was on ____________________, 20_____. Prior to moving into the premises, the Tenant(s) will ensure that their belongings and the containers, luggage and/or boxes they are packed in do not contain any pest(s), including, but not limited to, bedbugs or cockroaches. The Tenant(s) will notify the Landlord immediately of any pest issues found in the rental premise. The Tenant(s) will not bring in any abandoned items found as these items may be infested with pests. The Landlord will cover the cost of pest treatment unless the Landlord determines, in its sole and unfettered discretion, that the pest(s) in question were introduced to the premises by the Tenant(s), in which case, at the option of the Landlord, in its sole and unfettered discretion, the cost of all required pest treatment(s) shall be borne by and for the account of the Tenant(s). The Tenant(s) must prepare their suite as per the pest control guidelines given and as directed by the Landlord, prior to the treatment date. The Tenant(s) will allow entry to the Landlord and the Landlord’s contractor for the purpose of taking the necessary steps to control or treat pests, as well as any inspections required for effective pest control and treatments. The Tenant(s) acknowledges and understands that it may require more than one treatment as a part of effective treatment of pests. The Tenant(s) understands that even if no pests are present in the rental premise, it may be deemed necessary to treat the rental premise anyway as a measure of pest prevention. Failure to comply with these terms will be considered a substantial breach of this lease agreement, and may result in charges and/or immediate eviction. The Tenant(s) shall indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord against all losses, damages and/or costs, including, but not limited to, solicitor and own client costs and charges, including paralegal and civil enforcement charges, incurred in enforcing this clause, for any breach by the Tenant(s) of this clause and/or any introduction of pest(s) to the premises by the Tenant(s).

In accordance with the Residential Tenancy Act, the Tenant(s) must provide 30 days written notice to terminate the tenancy where the termination date specified on the Notice to Vacate cannot be a date earlier than the last day of the lease term specified in this Lease Agreement. 9. ADDITIONAL TERMS:(a) The monthly rental incentive/subsidies under this paragraph (the "Rental Subsidy") will be terminated in the event of any of the following:
(i) The Tenant(s) commits a substantial breach of the Lease Agreement as defined by the Residential Tenancies Act,
(ii) the rent is not paid on the first day of each month throughout the term, and
(iii) if during the term any two rent payments are returned for non-sufficient funds, and
(iv) the Tenant(s) no longer meets the Core Needs Income Threshold guidelines for eligibility for the subsidized suite.

(b) following termination of the Rental Subsidy, the Tenant(s) will be required to vacate the Apartment and can transfer to a different apartment in the Building at the rent set by the Landlord for that apartment, subject to availability. If a different apartment in the Building is not available, the Tenant(s) shall:
(i) transfer to an apartment in another building owned or managed by the Landlord and pay the rent set by the Landlord of that apartment, subject to availability; or
(ii) if the apartment in another building owned by the Landlord is not available or the Tenant(s) does not wish to transfer there, vacate the Apartment and terminate their tenancy(s) on termination of the Rental Subsidy.

The word "information" means the Tenant's personal, business and credit information. It includes information provided to the Landlord by the Tenant(s), including through the products and services the Tenant(s) uses, and information obtained from others.
In order for Boardwalk to comply with federal and/or provincial privacy legislation, I/we understand that all personal Information collected from me/us may be collected, used and disclosed by Boardwalk for the purpose of my/our application assessment, for the purpose of debt collection, to uphold and maintain the rules and regulations of the property, to evaluate my/our tenancy, to comply with applicable law and in the ordinary course of Boardwalk's business, including, but not limited to, any refinancing or potential sale of the property, and normal course dealings with providers of utilities and other like services.
For greater certainty, and without limiting the generality of the foregoing paragraph, the Tenant(s) agrees to the use of its information as follows:
i. Use of Information - The Landlord may use the Information to establish and serve the Tenant(s) as its customer, determine whether any products or services of the Landlord are suitable for the Tenant(s), or when required or permitted by law. The Landlord may share information with its third party providers, including, but not limited to, providers of utilities, lenders and potential purchasers of its assets; and
ii. Collection and Use of Information - THE LANDLORD MAY OBTAIN INFORMATION FROM THIRD PARTIES, INCLUDING THROUGH A CREDIT CHECK, AND VERIFY INFORMATION WITH THEM. THE TENANT(S) AUTHORIZES THOSE PARTIES TO GIVE THE LANDLORD INFORMATION.

{0}. AGREEMENT: During the contract Period, the Landlord grants to the Tenant, upon adhering to certain conditions:
a) the privilege, which is not assignable, to occupy the Room and use the Furnishings, effects and services in the Room;
b) the privilege to use, in common with Other Tenants, the Shared Amenities and the Furnishings, effects and services in the common areas of the Apartment;
c) the privilege to use, in common with other occupants of the Apartment, all designated common spaces located in the Building.

en-US.Reports.LeaseScheduleBRC.Label1to3(V1):
1. Notice of increase in rent
The Landlord shall not increase the rent payable under this Lease Agreement or recover any additional rent resulting from an increase unless the Landlord serves on the Tenant a written notice of the increase in rent at least 90 days before the date on which the increase is to be effective.
2. Termination for substantial breach by Tenant
If the Tenant commits a substantial breach of this Lease Agreement, the Landlord may apply to a court to terminate the tenancy or may terminate the tenancy by serving the Tenant with a notice at least 14 days before the day that the tenancy is to terminate.
3. Termination of tenancy for damage or assault
Notwithstanding section 3 above, if the Tenant has:
(a) done or permitted significant damage to the Apartment, the common areas of the Building or the property of which they form a part, or
(b) physically assaulted or threatened to physically assault the Landlord or another tenant,
the Landlord may apply to a court to terminate the tenancy or may terminate the tenancy by serving the Tenant with a notice at least 24 hours before the time that the tenancy is to terminate.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1AtoK(V1):
A. COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Apartment or Building by the Tenant(s). All Propane Tanks must be 1.5 meters (5 feet) clear space between the building windows and doors as per fire regulations.
B. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Apartment. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
C. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
D. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Apartment.
E. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
F. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Apartment and no locks are to be rekeyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
G. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
H. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
I. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus of the Building or of the Apartment. Tenant(s) is responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Apartment.
J. CHILDREN: Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
K. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1NtoP:
O. WIRING: No wires for electric lights, television, or radio connections or otherwise are to be introduced, nor the position of any existing wires altered without written consent from the Landlord.
P. GUESTS: Guests may not stay longer than 7 days without written consent from the Landlord. Guests may not park overnight on the property without permission from the Landlord; violators will be ticketed or towed. If handicapped parking is available, guests must display a valid handicapped placard. The Tenant(s) is responsible for the actions of their guests.
Q. APPLIANCES: Appliances are leased as part of the Apartment. The Tenant(s) will keep them in clean condition.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1M(V1): N. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke detectors monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke detector. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1M(V2): N. SMOKE ALARMS: Tenant(s) is responsible for testing smoke alarms monthly and for replacing batteries in the smoke alarm. Immediately report faulty detectors to the Landlord.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label1LSub(V1): Pets, such as cats, fish and certain breeds of small, quiet, well-mannered, non-aggressive dogs are allowed with written approval and permission from the Landlord unless the property is specified as a “non-pet” Building. Reptiles and other exotic animals are not allowed. Prior to purchasing any pet, contact the Landlord to ensure the type and breed of pet is acceptable.  Pet owners are fully responsible for cleanup and care and assume all liability for damage caused by their pets. When outside, Tenant(s) must be with the leashed pet at all times. PEST CONTROL: The Tenant(s) represents and warrants that they have not experienced any pest issues or infestations during any prior tenancy(s) or, in the alternative, if they have experienced pest issues or infestations during a prior tenancy(s), their last treatment was on ____________________, 20_____. Prior to moving into the premises, the Tenant(s) will ensure that their belongings and the containers, luggage and/or boxes they are packed in do not contain any pest(s), including, but not limited to, bedbugs or cockroaches. The Tenant(s) will notify the Landlord immediately of any pest issues found in the rental premise. The Tenant(s) will not bring in any abandoned items found as these items may be infested with pests. The Landlord will cover the cost of pest treatment unless the Landlord determines, in its sole and unfettered discretion, that the pest(s) in question were introduced to the premises by the Tenant(s), in which case, at the option of the Landlord, in its sole and unfettered discretion, the cost of all required pest treatment(s) shall be borne by and for the account of the Tenant(s). The Tenant(s) must prepare their suite as per the pest control guidelines given and as directed by the Landlord, prior to the treatment date. The Tenant(s) will allow entry to the Landlord and the Landlord’s contractor for the purpose of taking the necessary steps to control or treat pests, as well as any inspections required for effective pest control and treatments. The Tenant(s) acknowledges and understands that it may require more than one treatment as a part of effective treatment of pests. The Tenant(s) understands that even if no pests are present in the rental premise, it may be deemed necessary to treat the rental premise anyway as a measure of pest prevention. Failure to comply with these terms will be considered a substantial breach of this lease agreement, and may result in charges and/or immediate eviction. The Tenant(s) shall indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord against all losses, damages and/or costs, including, but not limited to, solicitor and own client costs and charges, including paralegal and civil enforcement charges, incurred in enforcing this clause, for any breach by the Tenant(s) of this clause and/or any introduction of pest(s) to the premises by the Tenant(s).

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.SK.Label12Sub:
The Tenant(s) shall be liable for the full term of the Lease Agreement.
Fixed-term tenancy: In the event that the Tenant(s) vacate the leased premises prior to the expiration of term, any and all funds received by the Tenant(s) as a move-in allowance and/or other rental incentive shall become immediately due and payable to the Landlord.
Periodic (monthly tenancy): The Tenant(s) may terminate the tenancy by providing at least one month’s written notice to the Landlord, specifying that the tenancy will end on a date that is the day before rent is payable under the lease. Written notice to terminate the tenancy must be received by the Landlord before the 1st of the month to be effective at noon on the last day of that month. Notices received on or after the 1st of the month will be considered late, and the Tenant(s) will be responsible for the rent of the following month unless the suite is re-rented before the end of that month.

{0}. SECURITY DEPOSIT: Security Deposit and interest shall be returned at the end of the tenancy. Any Security Deposit refundable (including interest) will be paid to all current Leaseholders. The Tenant(s) shall leave a forwarding address in writing with the Landlord. It is the Tenant(s) responsibility to arrange and be present at the move out inspection. The Security Deposit may be utilized by the Landlord as necessary to provide for (but not limited to):
(a) Repairing any damage or loss to the Apartment, Building or grounds and their contents caused by the Tenant(s) or their guests. (Burns and other marks on carpets, flooring, furniture, appliances and walls shall not be considered normal wear and tear.)
(b) Any cleaning and damages to the Apartment that are indicated on the move out inspection report. Dirt is not considered normal wear and tear.
(c) Professional carpet cleaning. If carpet is cleaned by the Landlord, the Tenant(s) will be charged accordingly.
(d) The Tenant(s) is responsible for the amount of any damages or cleaning costs in excess of the Security Deposit, any outstanding rent or any other obligations of the Tenant(s).
{1}. OTHER RULES: Tenant(s) will obey any rules posted regarding the use and care of the Building, parking lot, laundry room and other facilities such as swimming pool, playground, etc. that are provided for the use of the Tenant(s). The Landlord may from time to time make such other and further reasonable rules for the care and cleanliness of the Building and grounds and for the comfort and convenience of the Tenant(s). Guests shall obey such rules.

The word "information" means the Tenant's personal, business and credit information. It includes information provided to the Landlord by the Tenant(s), including through the products and services the Tenant(s) uses, and information obtained from others.
In order for Boardwalk to comply with federal and/or provincial privacy legislation, I/we understand that all personal Information collected from me/us may be collected, used and disclosed by Boardwalk for the purpose of my/our application assessment, for the purpose of debt collection, to uphold and maintain the rules and regulations of the property, to evaluate my/our tenancy, to comply with applicable law and in the ordinary course of Boardwalk's business, including, but not limited to, any refinancing or potential sale of the property, and normal course dealings with providers of utilities and other like services.
For greater certainty, and without limiting the generality of the foregoing paragraph, the Tenant(s) agrees to the use of its information as follows:
i. Use of Information - The Landlord may use the Information to establish and serve the Tenant(s) as its customer, determine whether any products or services of the Landlord are suitable for the Tenant(s), or when required or permitted by law. The Landlord may share information with its third party providers, including, but not limited to, providers of utilities, lenders and potential purchasers of its assets; and
ii. Collection and Use of Information - THE LANDLORD MAY OBTAIN INFORMATION FROM THIRD PARTIES, INCLUDING THROUGH A CREDIT CHECK, AND VERIFY INFORMATION WITH THEM. THE TENANT(S) AUTHORIZES THOSE PARTIES TO GIVE THE LANDLORD INFORMATION.

A. COMBUSTIBLES: No storage of combustibles or offensive goods, provisions or material, shall be kept in the Rental Unit or Building by the Tenant(s).
B. WALLS/FLOORS: Tenant(s) shall not use stickers or otherwise damage the walls, floors, ceiling or woodwork of the Rental Unit. Only small nails or hooks are to be used for wall hangings.
C. GARBAGE: All refuse shall be securely wrapped and tied before being placed in garbage dumpsters or in garbage chutes.
D. HALLS: The hallways, passages and stairs of the Building shall be used for no purpose other than going to and from the Rental Unit.
E. ALTERATIONS: No structural alterations, painting, papering, redecorating, installation of dishwashers, washers, or dryers shall be done without the written consent of the Landlord.
F. LOCKS: No additional locks shall be placed upon any door (inside or out) of the Rental Unit and no locks are to be re-keyed or changed without written consent of the Landlord.
G. WINDOWS: Tenant(s) will not shake, clean or hang any laundry, rugs, mats, cloth, flags, bedding, etc. from balconies or windows. Tenant(s) shall not place or hang anything out of windows or balconies. Only proper drapes and/or blinds may be hung on windows.
H. WATER: Water shall not be left running unless in actual use. To prevent flooding, shower curtains must be put inside the bathtub. Running toilets or any other water leakage must be reported at once; failure to do so will render the Tenant(s) responsible for damages.
I. FIXTURES: The Tenant(s), other occupants, and guests shall not interfere with the furnace, lights, or heating apparatus, of the Building or of the Rental Unit. Tenant(s) are responsible for replacing burnt out light bulbs and oven fuses within the Rental Unit.
J. CHILDREN: Children are not permitted to play in common areas.
K. LAUNDRY: Tenant(s) shall use the laundry room at their own risk. The Landlord assumes no responsibility for lost or damaged clothing while using laundry facilities. The laundry room as well as the appliances, tables and sinks shall be left in a clean and proper condition after use.

en-US.PublicWeb.ChangeLives-p1: Boardwalk Rental Communities Changing Lives Benefit is run in partnership with an international youth organization called Youth With A Mission (YWAM) and their Homes of Hope program. Boardwalk Rental Communities not only believes in continually working to improve the Communities in which we live, but also our global Communities. This is why, three times a year, we send approximately 150 Associates and Resident Members to Tijuana, Mexico to build houses for families in need. Each simple home is built by a team of 15-20 people, over a two-day period. The homes are then fully furnished with supplies donated by Boardwalk Rental Communities.
en-US.PublicWeb.ChangeLives-p2: While Boardwalk realizes that the nine homes we build per year will not solve Mexico's poverty or homelessness epidemic, Boardwalk does believe these are small global steps that make a significant local impact. Charity home building is a life-changing experience for both the families who receive the homes, as well as the Associates and Resident Members who build them. For the participants, the memories, relationships and appreciation of the blessings we enjoy in Canada are significant and last a lifetime.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-p1: Boardwalk Rental Communities would like to encourage other companies to consider participating in their own Homes of Hope trip. While many companies are interested in Boardwalk's Changing Lives Benefit, YWAM and Homes of Hope, but they also imagine that the planning and organization that goes into a home building trip must be incredibly time consuming and difficult.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-bullet2: Companies that encourage Associates to participate and invest in social causes see improved morale, productivity and leadership.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-bullet3: Investors, Stakeholders and Customers are more likely to support organizations who promote social responsibility.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-bullet4: A CWAM Changing Lives Benefit is an investment in your Associates, yourself and your company.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-p2: Companies With A Mission (CWAM) can provide companies with the logistical tools needed to organize and execute a Homes of Hope home building project. Boardwalk Rental Communities will provide you with a planning guide, which includes in-house marketing materials, suggested timelines, checklists, budgets and templates for participant information hand-outs.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-p3: Aside from the fact that you will be improving the lives of several families forever, there are many reasons to consider participating in a CWAM Changing Lives Benefit.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-bullet1: Corporate social responsibility is becoming increasingly important. Associates are more likely to stay with an organization if they believe in the social ideals represented and supported by the company.
en-US.PublicWeb.CompaniesWithAMission-p4: Any company can plan and take part in a home building project, and anyone who is physically able to handle light to moderate, unskilled labour, can participate in the build. Boardwalk's CWAM support is completely free of charge, as our motivation for starting this program is to encourage other companies to participate in this life- changing experience.

{1}. SUBLETTING/ASSIGNING: The Tenant(s) agrees that (s)he shall not assign or sublet for a price or otherwise, the whole or part of the demised premises herein without the consent of the Landlord. The Tenant(s) agrees that failing to obtain written consent is grounds for termination of the Lease Agreement.
{2}. TENANT NOTICES: Any notice respecting this Lease Agreement must be given to the Landlord, in writing, at the Landlord's rental office (or such other address as the Landlord may designate after this date) and to the Tenant(s) at the Apartment. The Tenant(s) agrees that any notice will only be effective upon date of actual receipt at the Landlord's rental office regardless of when mailed or sent by the Tenant(s).
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label7: {0}. ELEVATORS: The Landlord shall keep any elevators in the Building in a good state of repair but will not be responsible to the Tenant(s) or their guests for any loss or injury resulting from its misuse. The tenant acknowledges that the elevator may be taken out of service occasionally for various purposes such as maintenance, government required testing, emergencies etc. and that the Landlord will not be responsible for the Tenant(s) or their guests for any loss or injury, including any abatement of rent, resulting from any such shut down of the elevator. The Tenant acknowledges that in the event of a building emergency (e.g. fire, flood, power outage, etc.) elevator services will be unavailable and that the Landlord will not be responsible for the Tenant(s) or their guests for any loss or injury, including abatement of rent, resulting from any such shut down of the elevator.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label6(V1):
{0}. PARKING RULES: The Tenant(s) agrees to the following parking rules:
a) One month clear, written notice must be provided to cancel a parking stall.
b) No repairs or vehicle maintenance is to take place in parking areas or individual parking stalls.
c) Only vehicles, which are fully registered, licensed, insured and in operating condition may be parked in a stall. Trailers, recreational vehicles, motor homes and commercial vehicles (one tonne or greater), may not be parked in parking areas or individual parking stalls. Unauthorized vehicles will be towed at the owner's expense.
d) Do not follow, or let others follow you, into a parkade. Let the door close and then use your own key to enter.
e) Tenant(s) shall only park their vehicle in their designated stall. The Tenant(s) is responsible to remove vehicles parked in their designated stall not belonging to the Tenant(s). When required, parking passes must be clearly displayed in vehicle.
f) If the Tenant(s) breaches any of the provisions regarding parking the Tenant(s)’s vehicle will be removed from the property and the Tenant(s) will be liable for all costs associated with such removal.
g) The Tenant(s) acknowledges there is a risk of vehicle vandalism and theft. The Tenant(s) shall be responsible to lock their vehicle, fully close windows and remove all valuables when their vehicle is parked. The Landlord is not responsible for any damage to or theft of the Tenant(s) vehicle and its contents.
h) The Tenant(s) acknowledges and agrees that the Landlord shall not be responsible for any damage suffered by the Tenant(s) or their property, included, but not limited to, any vehicle owned, leased or operated by them, while in the parking lot of the Building (including the parking ramp) (the Parking Lot), no matter how such damage is caused, including, but not limited to damage caused by leaky pipes or other leaks in the Building. Further, the Tenant(s) agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord from any damage suffered by any person granted access to the Building by the Tenant(s) or their property, including, but not limited to, the Parking Lot, no matter how such damage is caused.
en-US.CommonTerms.SubsidyActivationEmailBody: Resident Member(s): {1}{0}City: {2}{0}Project: {3}{0}Building: {4}{0}Suite: {5}{0}Lease: {6}{0}{0}This unit is now a subsidized unit.  If it is unoccupied it may only be rented to clients sent by the subsidy agency noted on file.  If it is currently occupied a subsidy amount will be added by head office which will post to the ledger along with the rent charge.

{0}. PARKING RULES: The Tenant(s) agrees to the following parking rules:
a) One month clear, written notice must be provided to cancel a parking stall.
b) No repairs or vehicle maintenance is to take place in parking areas or individual parking stalls.
c) Only vehicles, which are fully registered, licensed, insured and in operating condition may be parked in a stall. Trailers, recreational vehicles, motor homes and commercial vehicles (one tonne or greater), may not be parked in parking areas or individual parking stalls. Unauthorized vehicles will be towed at the owner's expense.
d) Do not follow, or let others follow you, into a parkade. Let the door close and then use your own key to enter.
e) Tenant(s) shall only park their vehicle in their designated stall. The Tenant(s) is responsible to remove vehicles parked in their designated stall not belonging to the Tenant(s). When required, parking passes must be clearly displayed in vehicle.
f) If the Tenant(s) breaches any of the provisions regarding parking the Tenant(s)’s vehicle will be removed from the property and the Tenant(s) will be liable for all costs associated with such removal.
g) The Tenant(s) acknowledges there is a risk of vehicle vandalism and theft. The Tenant(s) shall be responsible to lock their vehicle, fully close windows and remove all valuables when their vehicle is parked. The Landlord is not responsible for any damage to or theft of the Tenant(s) vehicle and its contents.
h) The Tenant(s) acknowledges and agrees that the Landlord shall not be responsible for any damage suffered by the Tenant(s) or their property, included, but not limited to, any vehicle owned, leased or operated by them, while in the parking lot of the Building (including the parking ramp) (the Parking Lot), no matter how such damage is caused, including, but not limited to damage caused by leaky pipes or other leaks in the Building. Further, the Tenant(s) agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord from any damage suffered by any person granted access to the Building by the Tenant(s) or their property, including, but not limited to, the Parking Lot, no matter how such damage is caused.
{1}. STORAGE RULES: The Tenant(s) agrees to the following storage rules:
a) One calendar month clear, written notice must be provided to cancel a storage space.
b) No repairs or maintenance are to take place in storage space areas or individual storage spaces.
c) No hazardous substances, dangerous materials, firearms and/or ammunition may be kept in a storage space. Unauthorized substances or materials will be removed at the Owner’s expense.
d) Tenant(s) shall only store their property in their designated space.
e) If the Tenant(s) breaches any of the provisions regarding storage, the Tenant(s) property will be removed from the storage space and the Tenant(s) will be liable for all costs associated with such removal.
f) The Tenant(s) acknowledges and agrees that the Landlord shall not be responsible for any damages suffered by the Tenant(s) or their property, including, but not limited to any property stored in the storage space, no matter how such damage is caused, including but not limited to theft or damage caused by leaky pipes or other leaks in the Building. Further, the Tenant(s) agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord from any damages suffered by the Tenant(s) or any other person granted access to the Building by the Tenant(s) or their property, including, but not limited to the storage space, no matter how much damage is caused.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.BC.Label3to6:
9. UTILITIES: The Tenant(s) acknowledges that the supply of all utilities, including but not restricted to, cable television (wire line, satellite, and microwave), internet cable, telephone, natural gas, electric, heat and water, shall be provided by either the Landlord or suppliers selected by the Landlord and the Tenant(s) may not contract separately for such services without written consent of the Landlord, which consent may be withheld for any reason within the Landlord's discretion.
In the event the Landlord selects a third party supplier to provide one or more utilities to a Tenant, the Tenant(s) shall be responsible for transferring the applicable utilities into their name.
In addition to the utility charge, the Tenant shall be responsible for and covenants to pay any administration, service or other fee charged by said third party supplier, as well as an administration fee of $25.00 will be charged for all bills processed by the Landlord on behalf of the Tenant(s) (collectively the "Fees").
The Tenant(s) also acknowledges that where a utility is currently included in the rent the Landlord, in its sole discretion, may, at any time, choose to meter the Tenant's rented premises separately and transfer responsibility for payment of the utility (and any related Fees) directly to the Tenant based on the Tenant's own consumption.
10. MAINTENANCE REQUESTS: Subject to obtaining the prior consent (written) of the Tenant(s), the Landlord shall be permitted to enter the Apartment to handle a maintenance work order requested by the Tenant(s). Tenant(s) shall report all maintenance problems to the Landlord.
11. MAINTENANCE COSTS: The Tenant(s) shall be responsible for all cost of repairing any damage in the Apartment or in the common areas of the complex, which result from the neglect, or misuse of the facilities by the Tenant(s) or their guests. This includes costs to repair frozen and burst pipes, plumbing clogs, and insect infestation. Payment is due immediately.
12. LEGAL COSTS: The Tenant(s) shall pay for the Landlord’s solicitor and own client costs and charges, including paralegal and civil enforcement charges, lawfully and reasonably incurred in enforcing payment of any amount owed to the Landlord and in obtaining possession of the Apartment after default of a Tenant(s) or upon the expiration or earlier termination of this Lease or in performing any covenant, provision, or agreement of the Tenant(s) herein contained.

c) Only vehicles, which are fully registered, licensed, insured and in operating condition may be parked in a stall. Trailers, recreational vehicles, motor homes and commercial vehicles (one tonne or greater), may not be parked in parking areas or individual parking stalls. Unauthorized vehicles will be towed at the owner's expense.
d) Do not follow or let others follow you into a parkade. Let the door close and then use your own key to enter.
e) Tenant(s) shall only park their vehicle in their designated stall. The Tenant(s) must remove vehicles parked in the designated stall not belonging to the Tenant(s). When required, parking passes must be clearly displayed in vehicle.
f) If the Tenant(s) breaches any of the provisions regarding parking the Tenant(s)’s vehicle will be removed from the property and the Tenant(s) will be liable for all costs associated with such removal.
g) The Tenant(s) acknowledges there is a risk of vehicle vandalism and theft. The Tenant(s) shall be responsible to lock their vehicle, fully close windows and remove all valuables when their vehicle is parked. The Landlord is not responsible for any damage to or theft of the Tenant(s) vehicle and its contents.
h) The Tenant(s) acknowledges and agrees that the Landlord shall not be responsible for any damage suffered by the Tenant(s) or their property, included, but not limited to, any vehicle owned, leased or operated by them, while in the parking lot of the Building (including the parking ramp) (the Parking Lot), no matter how such damage is caused, including, but not limited to damage caused by leaky pipes or other leaks in the Building. Further, the Tenant(s) agree to indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord from any damage suffered by any person granted access to the Building by the Tenant(s) or their property, including, but not limited to, the Parking Lot, no matter how such damage is caused.

en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.AB.Label12Sub(V5): In the event that the Tenant(s) breaches the Lease Agreement by vacating the premises prior to the end of the fixed term portion of the Lease Agreement, the Tenant(s) shall pay to the Landlord total damages for the loss of the benefit of the Lease Agreement until the date the fixed term tenancy would have expired. The payment of this amount shall not affect any of the other obligations of the Tenant(s) hereunder and any liability for any damage to the Apartment. If the Tenant(s) vacates the premises herein described, prior to the expiration of the term of this Lease Agreement, or if late notice to vacate is provided, any and all money received by the Tenant(s) as a move-in-allowance, and/or rental bonus, and/or rental incentives shall be immediately due and payable to the Landlord. In accordance with the Residential Tenancy Act, the Tenant(s) must provide 30 days written notice to terminate the tenancy where the termination date specified on the Notice to Vacate cannot be a date earlier than the last day of the lease term specified in this Lease Agreement. Any notice given after midnight on the first day of the month to vacate on the last day of the month shall be considered late notice and, as a result, the tenant(s) will be responsible for next month’s rent unless the suite is re-rented prior to this date.

Sincerely,
The {7} Team

en-US.Reports.NoticeOfDefault.Body:
This Notice is to advise that your account is in default for the month of {0} in the amount of {1}.

As per the Residential Tenancies Act of Alberta sec. 21(a) "rent will be paid when due." As per your lease agreement you have signed with {2} the rent is due in full on the first of the month.

If you do not pay the full amount of outstanding arrears by Interac within 3 (three) days from the date of this Notice, your landlord may seek the following remedies:

   1. Action under the Civil Enforcement Act, or
   2. Action under the Residential Tenancies Act of Alberta to terminate the lease.

Please be advised that a late fee as per your Lease Agreement may be applied to all outstanding accounts. Payment of the late fee is not considered to be authorization allowing late payment of rent. Additionally, legal action may be initiated even after payment of your arrears, if it is found that you have been habitually late with payment of rent in your tenancy.

In hopes of avoiding legal action (which may result in additional costs to you in the approximate amount of $300 to $700), we ask you to please contact us immediately to arrange payment. We always strive to work with our Resident Members on mutual resolves and understand how some financial difficulties arise outside of our control. We look forward to working with you to resolve this situation.

en-US.Reports.NoticeOfDefaultQC.NoticeBody:
We have yet to receive your rent for the month of {0} in the amount of {1}. Please note that in accordance with the law the rent is payable in advance on the first day of each month (Art. 1903 C.C.Q.)).

Please be advised that should you not respect your obligation to pay your rent in accordance with the law, we will register a claim with the "Tribunal administratif du logement" to obtain payment, which may result in the termination of your lease. Also, we may request a ruling obliging you to pay on the 1st of the month should you persist in regularly paying your rent late.

An application filed with the Tribunal administratif du logement entails minimum fees of {2}, which you may be required to reimburse us. We invite you to avoid these additional costs by paying your rent in accordance with the law.

en-US.Reports.AbandonmentNotice.Body:
1. The Tenant(s) appear to have abandoned the Premises;
2. Rental arrears in the amount of {0} have not been paid; and,
3. The Landlord is concerned regarding the security and condition of the Premises;


TAKE NOTICE THAT THE LANDLORD, {1}, as a result of your abandonment of the Premises, hereby accepts your repudiation of the residential Lease Agreement.

The Landlord has changed the locks on the Premises in order to secure the Premises. Keys to the Premises are available to the tenant upon request. The Landlord can be contacted at {2}.

TAKE NOTICE THAT it is the landlord’s intention to dispose of any abandoned goods in accordance with Section 31(1) of the Residential Tenancies Act. If you have not abandoned the Premises, please call the Landlord within 48 hours. The Landlord will also be seeking against you, as Tenant, all damages, costs and expenses, including legal fees and disbursements, arising from your breach of the Lease Agreement.



Issue Date: {3} {4}, {5}.

en-US.CommonTerms.InvestorDistributionsSummary:
The Trust suspended its DRIP effective Feb 29, 2008. Notification to that effect was mailed to DRIP participants on Feb 15, 2008 (see Notification of Suspension).

The DRIP provided efficient and cost-effective equity to support the Trust's financing strategy. However, with its current liquidity and Normal Course Issuer Bid, the Trust no longer requires this source of funding. The Trust may reinstate the DRIP in the future if required to fund new investing activities. For more information on the Normal Course Issuer Bid, please see the information under the heading “Strategy for Growth-Normal Course Issuer Bid” in the Trust’s Renewal Annual Information Form, a copy of which may be found here.

The suspension of the DRIP does not affect regular distributions and Unitholders will continue to receive the regular distribution as declared.

Unitholders must consider the tax consequences of their past participation in the DRIP and the disclosure below should assist Unitholders in this regard.

Canadian Federal Tax considerations for DRIP participants

Electing distribution reinvestment option

Unitholders must consider the tax consequences of their past participation in the DRIP. Generally, where Participants elect to accumulate additional Units under the distribution reinvestment plan, the Participants reinvest their distributions in additional Units at approximately 97% of the Average Market Price. (A copy of the DRIP can be found on the Trust’s website at www.bwalk.com/Content/Investors/BREIT-DRIP.pdf for a description of the Plan.)

The Canada Revenue Agency (the "CRA") generally takes the position that under a DRIP where the fair market value of the Units acquired exceeds the purchase price, the difference is a benefit and must be included in the Participant's income for tax purposes. The cost of the Units acquired under the DRIP is the amount reinvested plus the amount of the benefit. The units acquired under the DRIP must be averaged with the cost of all other Units the Participant holds for the purpose of determining the adjusted cost base of all the Participant's Units. Capital gains or losses arising on a disposition of the Participant's Units will be measured by reference to the adjusted cost base of all the Participant's Units.

Distribution Reinvestment Plan ("DRIP")

To encourage participation and reward unitholders, investors registered in the Distribution Reinvestment Plan ("DRIP") will continue to receive a "bonus" distribution of additional Trust Units representing 3% of the amount of their cash distributions reinvested pursuant to the Plan.
en-US.Reports.LeaseSchedule.ON.Label1R(V4): R. FEES AND DISHONOURED PAYMENTS: A $20.00 fee will apply to any payment that is not honoured by the bank, in addition to interest due on late payments. Interest will be calculated monthly at the rate of 12% per annum on rent and other charges that become due but are not paid.
en-US.CommonTerms.InvestorIncomeTaxInformation:
The Declaration of Trust of Boardwalk Real Estate Investment Trust (“Boardwalk REIT”) provides that income and net taxable capital gains for purposes of the Tax Act will be allocated to Unitholders in the same proportion as distributions received by Unitholders.

Distributions may consist of the following for income tax purposes:

  • Distributions that are currently taxable, for which T3 supplementaries will be issued (to be shown as Box 26 of your T3 slip). This portion of distributions for income tax purposes will be treated as regular taxable income (and not treated as dividends or capital gains) to each Unitholder.
  • Distributions that are treated as a dividend received from a Canadian corporation. As such, it will be subject to a preferential tax treatment that all dividends from Canadian corporations receive (subject to the dividend tax credit).
  • Distributions (to be shown in Box 21 of your T3 slip) that represent your portion of capital gains allocated to you relating to gain on the sale of a property in the year, if any.

    Please note that of the portion reported as capital gains on your tax return, only 50% of this is included in the calculation of your taxable income. The non-taxable portion of the capital gain is not deducted from the adjusted cost base of your Units.

  • Distributions that are not currently taxable and will be treated for income tax purposes as a return of capital.distributions that are not currently taxable and will be treated for income tax purposes as a return of capital.

    Accordingly, this currently non-taxable portion will reduce the adjusted cost base of the Units owned by each Unitholder. If, after deducting the return of capital portion, your adjusted cost base of your Units is a positive amount, no portion of the return of capital will be taxable. If, however, after deducting the return of capital, your adjusted cost base of your Units is a negative amount, you will realize a capital gain equal to the negative amount and your resultant adjusted cost base of your Units will be nil.

Unitholders should consult their tax advisors with respect to any questions they may have concerning tax matters.

en-US.CommonTerms.InvestorRealEstateTrustSumma: Boardwalk REIT strives to provide Canada’s friendliest communities and currently owns and operates more than 200 communities with over 33,000 residential units totaling over 28 million net rentable square feet. Boardwalk’s principal objectives are to provide its Residents with the best quality communities and superior customer service, while providing Unitholders with sustainable monthly cash distributions, and increase the value of its Trust Units through selective acquisitions, dispositions, development, and effective management of its residential multi-family communities. Boardwalk REIT is vertically integrated and is Canada’s leading owner/operator of multi-family communities with 1,700 Associates bringing Residents home to properties located in Alberta, Saskatchewan, Ontario, and Quebec.

The reason for giving this notice is as follows:

en-US.Reports.NSFWarning.BodyV2:
Please be advised that your payment for {0} has been returned, uncleared, to us by the bank for the following reason:

Cheque Returned. ({1})

Thank you for promptly resubmitting payment to the rental office.

A {2} administration fee for this returned cheque has been charged to your account.

Please note, should we receive three (3) returned cheques in a six (6) month period, chequing privileges will be revoked. Should this happen, all future payments must be in the form of a certified cheque or money order. Once payment has been received (in full) on or before the first day of each month for the next six (6) months, cheques will be accepted for rental charges.

Should you have any questions or concerns, please contact your Customer Service Representative at {3}.

Thank you for your tenancy with {4}.

Sincerely,

en-US.PublicWeb.BoardwalkAngels_p6: Boardwalk has been involved in the Changing Lives Benefit for more than 10 years. Our partners there once said, “It is easy to tell a Boardwalk home because each has a heart beside the entrance.
en-US.PublicWeb.BoardwalkAngels_p5: Boardwalk includes Resident Members in these humanitarian pursuits because we believe we can do so much more together.
en-US.PublicWeb.WhyBoardwalk_p1: Over the course of 30 years, our very name – Boardwalk Rental Communities - has reflected our vision, a vision we continue to share with our team of Associates and Resident Members today. We believe by strengthening the bonds of community and family across all our Associates and Resident Members, we are better able to bring our Resident Members home and make Boardwalk an inviting and inclusive place to live where Residents feel a real sense of belonging.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurPromise_p2: We are committed to fostering respectful, friendly, and supportive communities that engender a very real sense of family and belonging.
en-US.PublicWeb.WhyBoardwalk_p2: Today, we continue to draw inspiration from our early days, taking pride in who we are, what we do, and why as we immerse ourselves in a culture that welcomes home all who wish to be a part of a community built on love and mutual respect.
en-US.PublicWeb.WhyBoardwalk_p3: Together, Boardwalk’s Resident Members and Associates believe in creating caring communities where our daily living experiences are best defined by a common understanding of “why” - “why” we are who we are and “why” we do what we do. This understanding is best articulated in our Golden Foundation: Treat others as you would like to be treated; Be Good; Love Community; and, Have Fun. Whether you live or work with us, you are an integral part of our Boardwalk family and, together, we all bring our shared vision of building better communities to life. We remain committed to this core purpose because we have learned that nourishing long-terms relationships with (and between) our Associates and Resident Members is the best way to meet the needs of our communities and build strong and thriving communities
en-US.PublicWeb.BoardwalkAngels_p4: A few examples include our Changing Lives Benefit (Homes of Hope builds), numerous Feed the Homeless events across Canada, the Quebec Cancer Society Daffodil Campaign, Samaritan’s Purse Shoebox Campaign, Saskatoon’s Easter Seal Drop Zone, Boardwalk’s Walk for Wellspring in London and many more.

In order for Boardwalk to comply with federal and/or provincial privacy legislation, I/we understand that all personal information collected from me/us may be collected, used and disclosed by Boardwalk for the purpose of my/our application assessment, for the purpose of debt collection, to uphold and maintain the rules and regulations of the property, to evaluate my/our tenancy, to comply with applicable law and in the ordinary course of Boardwalk’s business, including, but not limited to, any refinancing or potential sale of the property, and normal course dealings with providers of utilities and other like services.

For the Province of Ontario, upon Boardwalk completing the processing of the offer to lease and confirming the applicants as being approved, the Tenancy Agreement/ tenancy is deemed to be formed and that the acknowledged amount received of the sum of $ ________ as a deposit shall be deemed to be a last month’s rent deposit as defined by the Ontario Residential Tenancies Act. In the event the applicants/tenants do not take possession of the rental on the commencement date as agreed upon or fail to execute the Lease Agreement when presented for execution, the last month’s rent deposit may be retained and applied to the rent charged for the month period following the agreed upon commencement date.

en-US.PublicWeb.BringingYouHome_p1: At Boardwalk Rental Communities, our daily interactions are centered on two core principles: our commitment to ensuring Resident Members enjoy superior customer service in our high quality rental communities and that they are proud to call Boardwalk home.
en-US.PublicWeb.BringingYouHome_p2: Through our delivery of quality customer service and the pride we take in who we are, what we do and why, our core principles and values continue to define us as an organization that truly cares about those we serve.  We believe the Boardwalk Community is built on love, where our Resident Members feel at home and have a very real sense of belonging.  We believe a sense of home, comfort, and belonging encapsulates what community means to us. Through our words and actions, we define who we are and what it is like to live in a Boardwalk community where our Associates and Resident Members treat each other as family.
en-US.PublicWeb.MissionVisionValues_P1: Boardwalk Rental Communities is proud of, and committed to, our Mission, Vision and Values. The following statements govern the decisions and policies our company makes and determine the way we treat each other, our Associates, our Resident Members, and our communities.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurVision_li3: Maintain building exteriors and landscaping, thereby increasing "curb appeal"; have well-kept common areas and ensure our homes are clean and well maintained.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurVision_li4: Maintain a balance between our Resident Members, Associates, Unitholders, communities and families.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurVision_li5: Nurture and promote a learning environment where our Associates' skills and capabilities grow with the needs of both the company and our Resident Members, and accept that these needs will be constantly evolving and improving the definition of "Rental Communities".
en-US.PublicWeb.OurVision_li6: Provide our Associates access to the latest tools and technology.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurValues_p1: Integrity: We will be honest, accountable, transparent, objective, constructive, respectful and trusting in our dealings with others, appreciating their views and differences.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurValues_p2: Our Associates: We will provide a safe and respectful work environment that attracts, supports, develops and recognizes high-performing and innovative team members while striving to maintain a strong work-life balance.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurPartners_p2: Caring for each other, and respecting one another, always, is how we can make our communities places to truly call home.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurValues_p3: Teamwork: We will effectively work as a team, appreciating and benefiting from each other's unique talents and skills in an open environment while recognizing that the team's successes are our successes.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurValues_p4: Customer Service: We will promptly respond to Resident Member concerns and needs with thoughtfulness, compassion and innovation while at the same time striving to develop proactive solutions through a support network and a positive service attitude.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurValues_p5: Social Responsibility: We will contribute to our communities and encourage our Associates and Resident Members to also contribute in ways that reflect our Golden Foundation, balancing our needs with those of others.
en-US.PublicWeb.HavingFun_p1: Our Communities are more than just a place to live: every Boardwalk community is a place where we want our Resident Members to feel a sense of family and community, a place they can call home. Boardwalk believes in nurturing relationships within our Boardwalk family and we do this by engaging in various events where our Resident Members and Associates come together. Building Better

en-US.PublicWeb.HavingFun_p2: Communities for us all to live and work in is our purpose, one we can achieve together by giving back to our communities, or by simply coming together in the spirit of fun.
en-US.PublicWeb.OurPartners_p1: Boardwalk believes in, and is committed to, building better communities where we encourage all Resident Members and Associates to be respectful, supportive, friendly, and proud to be a part of the Boardwalk family. Boardwalk believes every act of giving inspires another, and it is through these acts of giving, both big and small, we create communities in which we are all happy to live and work.

en-US.PublicWeb.InsuranceRequirements_bullet_0: Unless waived by notice in writing given by BOARDWALK to the Contractor before the Contractor commences supply, the Contractor shall ensure that the Contractor’s Liability Insurance contains the following provisions:
The Contractor shall immediately notify BOARDWALK in writing if Contractor’s Insurance is cancelled or materially changed.
en-US.PublicWeb.InsuranceRequirements_img_alt: Apartments For Rent | Boardwalk Rental Communities
en-US.PublicWeb.InsuranceRequirements_bullet_0: The Contractor shall not take any steps to supply any goods or services to BOARDWALK until the Contractor has first provided BOARDWALK with such written proof as BOARDWALK may reasonably require that the Contractor has obtained and paid for Contractor’s Insurance.
The Contractor shall maintain Contractor’s Insurance for the duration of any supply contracts between BOARDWALK and the Contractor, including any extension thereof.

The Contractor shall, within three (3) business days after receiving BOARDWALK’s written request, provide BOARDWALK with such written proof as BOARDWALK may reasonably require, that the Contractor has current Contractor’s Insurance.

If for any reason the Contractor’s coverage under the Contractor’s Insurance lapses, or the Contractor fails to provide BOARDWALK with written proof of insurance, BOARDWALK may, in its absolute discretion, immediately terminate any supply contracts BOARDWALK may have with the Contractor.
en-US.PublicWeb.InsuranceRequirements_sub_bull: Comprehensive general liability insurance in forms and with Insurers satisfactory to BOARDWALK, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld, with a coverage limit not less than two million dollars ($2,000,000) inclusive limit for any one occurrence, on any contract (the “Contractor’s Liability Insurance”); and
Owned Automobile Liability Insurance. (collectively the “Contractor’s Insurance”)
BOARDWALK shall be named as an Additional Insured to the Contractor’s Liability Insurance policy;
The Contractor’s employees, agents and sub-contractors shall be named as Additional Insureds to the Contractor’s Liability Insurance policy;
The Contractor’s Liability Insurance shall not be cancelled or materially changed without BOARDWALK first being provided with thirty (30) days’ written notice;
A waiver of subrogation against BOARDWALK;
A “drift” coverage extension, if the Contractor is to, in the course of supply to BOARDWALK, use any product that involves outdoor spraying;
BOARDWALK shall be provided, by the Contractor’s Liability Insurance provider, with a notice in the event of any policy’s reduction in insurance coverage and/or cancellation.
en-US.PublicWeb.WCBRequirements_img_alt: Apartments For Rent | Boardwalk Rental Communities
en-US.PublicWeb.WCBRequirements_Text: The Contractor shall submit, with their offer, a clearance letter from the provincial Compensation Board for Workers (WCB, WSIB, CSST), stating that the Contractor has an account in good standing with the province’s Compensation Board for Workers; the Contractor must hold such accounts in all Provinces that the Contractor provides service to BOARDWALK. This letter will be in the name and account number of the Contractor who has offered to provide services and supplies under this agreement. The account must include personal coverage for, inter alia, all partners, proprietors or directors of the firm, company or corporation who are present, or may have cause to be present, at the work site. The Contractor shall ensure compliance by both the Contractor itself and sub-contractors with the requirements of the provinces Workers’ Compensation Legislation and all applicable rules and regulations thereunder, throughout the term of the Agreement. BOARDWALK reserves the right to request a clearance letter at any time. Should the Contractor’s provincial Compensation Board for Workers (WCB,WSIB, CSST) account not be in good standing at the time of payment, BOARDWALK reserves the right to hold back full payment until such time as clearance is received, or hold back the amount of the arrears so that payment may be made to the provincial Compensation Board for Workers (WCB, WSIB, CSST) on the Contractor’s behalf, where such liability exists.

Christie Point is a secluded piece of ocean-front heaven. We are blessed with beautiful vistas and abundant wildlife.

Residents of Christie Point share a common vision for our community. We are a peaceful and welcoming community of individuals who work together to preserve the beauty and serenity of Christie Point. We encourage and support the creation of beautiful personal and community gardens. We encourage resident input into the management of Christie Point, as well as activities that bring the members of our community together.

Our residents watch out for and help each other - just as any good family would do. Our residents respect the rights of other community members. In doing these things, Christie Point is our own personal piece of heaven.

en-US.PublicWeb.job_DetailPage_Intro:
Boardwalk Rental Communities:

Over 30 years ago Boardwalk Rental Communities began with a clear Mission, to provide Resident Members with quality rental communities, a vision for the future and a commitment to five core values. These Mission, Vision and Values, coupled with Boardwalk’s Golden Foundation, have helped to create a team of Associates who are committed to building better communities. Whether it is a local Community or a global one, Boardwalk Associates approach it with the same foundation and aim to build and strengthen it.

At Boardwalk Rental Communities, we strive to become your “Employer of Choice”. We are committed to engaging our Associates through mutual respect, trust and honesty as well as following our Golden Foundation which is to “Treat others as you would like to be treated", “Be Good”, “Love Community” and “Have Fun”.

We believe that we are providing our Associates with a workplace that they can call home; a place where coming to work isn’t a chore. We continually look for new and innovative ways to engage our Associates not only in their day-to-day work, but also ways to get involved in the Community and provide opportunities to give back.

Boardwalk is seeking individuals who have a positive attitude, are energetic and as passionate as we are in building communities and serving our Resident Members. You must be self-driven, productive and have the capacity to deliver high-quality results. Working within a team is essential to your success at Boardwalk.
en-US.PublicWeb.Furture_Project_Landingpage_We:
Boardwalk Rental Communities is proud to announce a brand new modern style apartment building that will be opening in the community of Normanview West in Regina, Saskatchewan.

Situated within the current Boardwalk property of Pines of Normanview, Pines Edge is a beautiful four-storey building featuring charming landscapes and wonderful outdoor spaces.

1062 Dorothy Street Regina, Saskatchewan

Please visit the leasing office at Pines of Normanview at 1060 Dorothy Street for viewings and inquiries.

We will begin showings on Dec. 5, 2015 for Residents to move-in in early February 2016.

306-751-2255

Join our mailing list to get Open House information and to receive a pre-lease application!
en-US.PublicWeb.Furture_Project_Landingpage_Su: The bright and spacious suites of Pines Edge will be equipped with all the features you’d expect in a superior modern rental accommodation.
Balcony or Patio - Step outside. From top to bottom the spacious balcony or patio makes every unit a suite with a view
Heated Underground Parking - A rare luxury, Pines Edge has a safe and secure space to keep your vehicle warm through the winter months
Twin Elevators - Quick and efficient, ride one of two elevators to your new home without having to carry groceries up long flights of stairs
Modern, High Quality Finishings - From cabinets to countertops, experience the superior details that put Boardwalk buildings ahead of the pack.
Six Appliances - Stylish and advanced, each suite comes prepared for living with a new washer, dryer, dishwasher, fridge, range oven and hood fan.
In Suite Laundry - No coins required. Enjoy the comfort and convenience of having a private washer and dryer in your home.

en-US.PublicWeb.about_BoardwalkAngels_p_8: At Boardwalk we firmly believe that Community is not a celebration of me and mine or you and yours. Instead, Community is service with a common vision, a consistent voice and a collective action. Community is a celebration of we and our. Embracing this vision with humility, Boardwalk Rental Communities recognizes how fortunate we are to be able to serve and are proud of our Angel initiatives.
en-US.PublicWeb.RS_Span: Boardwalk Retirement Communities offer a lifestyle free of many everyday living responsibilities such as meal preparation, housekeeping and house maintenance. We strive to provide healthy living, excellent service, superior food and the peace of mind that comes with assisted living. Our Resident Members enjoy comfortable, senior-oriented apartment living in many major Canadian Cities. Find your new home with Boardwalk Retirement Communities today!

en-US.PublicWeb.Furture_Project_Landingpage_Co:
Located in the beautiful family-friendly community of Normanview West, Pines Edge will be close to amenities Boardwalk Residents desire.


Pines Edge will be steps from elementary schools and daycares, parks and playgrounds, as well as stores and shopping. A well-known community bike path runs along Wascana Creek through beautiful Ruth M. Buck Park. Baseball diamonds and outdoor rinks also provide great places for the community to gather and children to play.


Nearby Normanview Shopping Centre features banks, restaurants, retailers and a movie theatre to cater to all Residents’ commercial and shopping needs. Normanview West also has easy access to the Ring Road for quick commuting.

en-US.PublicWeb.commercial_Leasing_p_1: If you are looking for the perfect place for your business we can help. We lease commercial and retail office space in cities across Canada. Whether you need a large, open and spacious showroom or a cozy boutique, we can offer exactly what you’re looking for.
en-US.PublicWeb.commercial_Leasing_p_2: Many of our commercial and retail spaces are on street-level and in highly visible, high traffic locations, which will draw your customers to you. Since most of our spaces are on the ground floor of our largest residential apartment buildings, you’ll enjoy high volume, customer-based traffic from the building above, as well as from passing pedestrians. If you are looking for a stand-alone commercial building, we’ve got that available too.
en-US.PublicWeb.commercial_Leasing_p_3: Our commercial space is ideal for services such as medical clinics, salons and professional offices, as well as businesses such as cafes, restaurants, and convenience stores. We also offer rooftop leases for telecommunications antennas!
en-US.PublicWeb.commercial_Leasing_p_5: Our Commercial Leasing Associates will answer your questions and respond to your needs immediately, efficiently, and professionally.  Contact the Commercial Leasing team today.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_1: Boardwalk Rental Communities began as a private company in 1984 when brothers Sam and Van Kolias purchased a 16-unit walk-up in Calgary using a down payment guaranteed by their father. They immediately set to work renovating the interior and exterior of the building. Their hard work paid off. The newly improved building quickly filled with Residents. Within six months, the brothers sold the fully occupied property to fund newly created jobs and further acquisitions of similar run-down properties.
en-US.PublicWeb.project_detail_Note1: *Information, suite availability and rental rates are subject to change.
en-US.PublicWeb.project_detail_Note2: Rental rates listed are net and include current incentives when applicable. Suites and floor plans may not be as shown.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_p_1: After starting with just one building over 30 years ago, Boardwalk Rental Communities has grown to include over 225 properties across Canada. We strive to be one of Canada’s friendliest landlords and provide our Resident Members with the best quality rental communities and superior customer service.. It has been a long and exciting road from one property to 225 and we could not have done it without our dedicated team of Associates.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_2: In January of 1994, Boardwalk Rental Communities went public on the Alberta Stock Exchange, giving Associates an opportunity to own part of the company for just $0.0675 per share (accounting for two stock splits). During that year, Boardwalk purchased more than 1,000 apartment units in Calgary and Edmonton, Alberta.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_3: In 1996, Boardwalk Rental Communities expanded into Saskatchewan and in 1999 Boardwalk began expanding into Central Canada by buying apartment buildings in Ontario. In 2002, Boardwalk expanded further and established a presence in Quebec.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_4: In 2004, with 31,780 suites, Boardwalk Rental Communities converted to a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT). A REIT is a corporation that is not taxed because it distributes most of its net taxable income to shareholders.
en-US.PublicWeb.emailUs_lable_HowToContact: How would you like us to contact you?
en-US.PublicWeb.careers_Careers_p_1: There are many reasons why you should consider a new career with Boardwalk Rental Communities:
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_6: Today, with 32,947 suites, Boardwalk Rental Communities has earned the reputation of being the friendliest owner and operator of multi-family residential apartments in Canada. Boardwalk currently employs more than 1,500 skilled and dedicated Associates and provides superior customer service to more than 55,000 Resident Members across four provinces in Canada.
en-US.PublicWeb.about_OurStory_Timeline_p_5: In 2012 Boardwalk commenced its very first development project. This building sits on excess land that we own. Boardwalk was given a rent subsidy grant from the Province of Alberta for approximately half of the cost of the development project.  In this partnership, Boardwalk committed to provide 54 of the 109 units in this building as affordable, with rental rates 10% below CMHC market rents for 20 years.
en-US.PublicWeb.careers_OurTeam_p_2: Boardwalk Rental Communities is proud of our incredible team of more than 1,600 Associates.  We hire only the best candidates and then train and support our Associates to provide the best in customer service.  We encourage our staff to live by our Mission, Vision and Values every day.  As our Chairman and CEO, Sam Kolias says, “Our Values can only be achieved through our Associates, not by our bricks and mortar”.
en-US.PublicWeb.careers_OurTeam_p_3: At Boardwalk, we are proud of our corporate culture and are always working towards strengthening and building our Boardwalk Community. Working at Boardwalk is working amongst friends and family. We always support and encourage each and every one of our Associates to reach their full potential.
